script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'sota-architecture-deepdive',
  description: 'Fetch the missing top-6 artifacts and reverse-engineer the architecture of every system above us on the GPT-5.5 board',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Acquire', detail: 'fetch the missing top-6 submission artifacts from HuggingFace/GitHub' },
    { title: 'Dissect', detail: 'one deep agent per system: architecture, design, published record' },
    { title: 'Verify', detail: 'adversarially check every architecture claim against a primary source' },
  ],
}

const CORPUS = '/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus'
const BOARD = `THE LEADERBOARD ON OUR EVALUATOR (GPT-5.5) — this is the ONLY board that matters to us:
  cellcog-max 55.78 | WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612 54.78 | bodhi 54.07 | lunon 53.51 | dalpha-deepresearch 53.10
  sourcery 51.17 | gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch 49.98 (== the "reference", so parity = 0.50) | openai-deepresearch 47.84
  POLARIS (us) 43.82 | perplexity-Research 43.05 | grok-deeper-search 41.22
NOTE: the widely-cited "ADORE 52.65" is on NEITHER board — treat it as unverified. The legacy Gemini-2.5-Pro
board (qianfan 58.03, ZTE 57.27, ...) is a DIFFERENT evaluator and its numbers are NOT comparable to ours.
We already hold 100-task artifacts for: cellcog, tavily, onyx, deepinsight, baidu-qianfan (in ${CORPUS}).`

const ACQ = {
  type: 'object',
  required: ['fetched', 'notes'],
  properties: {
    fetched: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'object', properties: { system: { type: 'string' }, path: { type: 'string' }, tasks: { type: 'number' }, source_url: { type: 'string' } } } },
    notes: { type: 'string', description: 'What could NOT be fetched and why. Be explicit — a missing artifact is a finding.' },
  },
}

phase('Acquire')
const acquire = await agent(
  `Acquire the DeepResearch Bench submission ARTIFACTS (the generated reports, raw_data jsonl) for the systems we
are missing. We have cellcog, tavily, onyx, deepinsight, baidu-qianfan. We are MISSING the other top-6 on our board:
**WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612, bodhi, lunon_full100_FINAL.submission, dalpha-deepresearch, sourcery** — and also
**openai-deepresearch** and **gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch** (the reference), which we want as calibration points.

${BOARD}

WHERE TO LOOK: the benchmark's HuggingFace org/dataset (muset-ai / DeepResearch-Bench, the leaderboard Space's
backing dataset repo — leaderboard Spaces usually store submissions in a dataset repo), the GitHub repo
github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench (data/test_data/raw_data/), and any HF dataset mirrors. Use huggingface_hub
/ curl / git clone as needed. Save each as ${CORPUS}/<system>.jsonl.

Verify each file: 100 lines, each with a 'prompt' and an 'article'/'content'. Report line counts.
If a submission is NOT publicly downloadable, say so plainly — do NOT fabricate a path. A confirmed absence is
a real finding (it means we can only reverse-engineer that system from its published score, not its text).`,
  { schema: ACQ, effort: 'high', label: 'acquire:top6-artifacts', phase: 'Acquire' }
)
log(`acquired ${acquire?.fetched?.length ?? 0} new artifact sets — ${acquire?.notes?.slice(0, 160)}`)

const ARCH = {
  type: 'object',
  required: ['system', 'has_paper', 'architecture', 'composition_method', 'evidence', 'what_we_can_steal'],
  properties: {
    system: { type: 'string' },
    has_paper: { type: 'boolean' },
    architecture: { type: 'string', description: 'End-to-end: planning, retrieval, memory, outline, composition, revision. Concrete.' },
    composition_method: { type: 'string', description: 'THE KEY QUESTION: how is the REPORT WRITTEN? one pass or many? section-scoped or global context? outline-first? revision loops? critic? How is synthesis produced?' },
    performance: { type: 'string', description: 'Its scores per dimension on OUR (GPT-5.5) board; how it differs from the pack.' },
    evidence: { type: 'string', description: 'URLs / file:line of artifacts measured. NO claim without a source.' },
    unknowns: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'What is genuinely NOT public. Say so rather than speculate.' },
    what_we_can_steal: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
  },
}

phase('Dissect')
const SYSTEMS = [
  { k: 'cellcog', s: 'cellcog-max (55.78, RANK 1 on our judge)' },
  { k: 'whalecloud', s: 'WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612 (54.78, rank 2)' },
  { k: 'bodhi', s: 'bodhi (54.07, rank 3)' },
  { k: 'lunon', s: 'lunon (53.51, rank 4)' },
  { k: 'dalpha', s: 'dalpha-deepresearch (53.10, rank 5)' },
  { k: 'sourcery', s: 'sourcery (51.17, rank 6)' },
  { k: 'openai_gemini', s: 'openai-deepresearch (47.84) AND gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch (49.98, the reference) — the two systems we can most directly calibrate against' },
]
const dissected = await parallel(SYSTEMS.map(x => () =>
  agent(`Reverse-engineer the ARCHITECTURE of: **${x.s}**

${BOARD}

DO BOTH of these:
1. THE PUBLISHED RECORD. Search hard: arXiv, GitHub, company blog, HF model/dataset card, tech report, WeChat/Zhihu
   posts (several of these are Chinese teams — search Chinese-language sources too: 深度研究, 智能体, 报告生成).
   Is there a paper? Code? A tech report? If there is genuinely NOTHING public, SAY SO — do not invent an architecture.
2. THE ARTIFACT. If we have its reports in ${CORPUS} (or the Acquire phase just fetched them), READ THEM and measure
   them across MANY tasks, not just task 72. Reverse-engineer the generation process from the text: section/subsection
   structure, paragraph length distribution, whether it uses bullets/tables (and whether that varies BY TASK GENRE),
   how it cites, whether it names studies in prose, how it handles conflicting evidence, whether it has a dedicated
   synthesis/tensions section, whether the writing looks single-pass or multi-step, whether there is evidence of a
   revision/critique pass.

THE QUESTION THAT MATTERS MOST: **how does it COMPOSE the report, and how does it produce SYNTHESIS?**
We are an extraction-grounded fact conveyor — we span-verify every sentence, which forbids cross-source inference.
We score insight 0.4238 (weight 0.32, our worst). Find out what these systems do INSTEAD.

Prefer primary sources. Mark anything unverified. We were burned tonight by a confidently-quoted stale number.`,
    { schema: ARCH, effort: 'high', label: `dissect:${x.k}`, phase: 'Dissect' })
))
const D = dissected.filter(Boolean)
log(`dissected ${D.length}/7 | with papers: ${D.filter(d => d.has_paper).length}`)

phase('Verify')
const V = {
  type: 'object', required: ['verdict', 'reasoning'],
  properties: {
    verdict: { type: 'string', enum: ['CONFIRMED', 'PARTIAL', 'UNSUPPORTED', 'FALSE'] },
    reasoning: { type: 'string' },
    corrected: { type: 'string' },
  },
}
const checked = await parallel(D.map(d => () =>
  agent(`Adversarially verify this reverse-engineered architecture claim. Default to UNSUPPORTED if you cannot
confirm it from a PRIMARY source (paper, repo, official doc) or from the artifact text itself.

SYSTEM: ${d.system}
HAS PAPER: ${d.has_paper}
ARCHITECTURE CLAIMED: ${d.architecture}
COMPOSITION METHOD CLAIMED: ${d.composition_method}
EVIDENCE CITED: ${d.evidence}

Check: does the cited source actually say this? Is it current (2026)? Is an "architecture" being inferred from
output text and then stated as fact? Inference from artifacts is LEGITIMATE but must be LABELLED as inference,
not presented as documented design. Flag any place that line is crossed.`,
    { schema: V, effort: 'high', label: `verify:${d.system?.slice(0, 20)}`, phase: 'Verify' })
    .then(v => ({ system: d.system, verdict: v.verdict, reasoning: v.reasoning, corrected: v.corrected, dissection: d }))
))
const C = checked.filter(Boolean)
log(`verified: ${C.filter(c => c.verdict === 'CONFIRMED').length} confirmed, ${C.filter(c => c.verdict === 'UNSUPPORTED' || c.verdict === 'FALSE').length} rejected`)

return { acquire, systems: C }

fetched.system: PER-TASK JUDGE SCORES: race_result.txt for all 10 systems + raw_results.jsonl for 9 of 10 (cellcog-max per-task = 404, aggregate only)
upstream GPT-5.5 leaderboard CSV - diffs IDENTICAL to our leaderboard_gpt55_judge.csv (provenance confirmed)

acquire.notes:
EVERYTHING REQUESTED WAS ACQUIRED. All 5 missing top-6 systems + both calibration points are public. Zero fabricated paths; every file is HTTP 200 + validated.

WHERE THEY WERE HIDING (the unlock): the leaderboard Space muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard has TWO parallel data dirs. `data/` = LEGACY Gemini-2.5-Pro board (30 article files; qianfan 58.03, ZTE 57.27 — NOT our board). `data_gpt55/` = OUR GPT-5.5 board, a separate directory holding exactly the 10 systems on our leaderboard. Searching only `data/raw_data/` (or the GitHub repo, or the HF Dataset repo) makes the 5 missing systems look absent — they are not. HF search for "bodhi"/"lunon"/"dalpha"/"sourcery"/"WhaleCloud" returns nothing because they exist ONLY as files inside the Space, not as named repos.

VERIFICATION (all 10 pass): 100 lines each; keys `id`/`prompt`/`article`; ids exactly 1..100; prompts match GitHub reference.jsonl 100/100 for every system (so task IDs align across all systems); zero empty articles. Provenance proven not assumed: cellcog-max/race_result.txt reads Overall 0.5578 w/ sub-scores matching our CSV exactly, and upstream data_gpt55/leaderboard.csv diffs IDENTICAL to our leaderboard_gpt55_judge.csv.

FINDING 1 — WE HOLD THE WRONG #1. Our existing /drb_corpus/cellcog.jsonl is byte-identical (sig d2d06cf641c9) to the LEGACY `drb_cellcog_max` (Gemini board, 56.67, avg 6081 words). The real #1 on OUR board, `cellcog-max` (55.78), is a DIFFERENT, much longer submission: avg 10,907 words (~1.8x longer). We have been reverse-engineering the wrong artifact. Correct file now at gpt55_board/cellcog-max.jsonl. (Legacy drb_cellcog = avg 5840 words, sig 6f9acf29e6ba — a third distinct variant.)

FINDING 2 — 4 OF OUR 5 EXISTING ARTIFACTS ARE OFF-BOARD. tavily, onyx, deepinsight, baidu-qianfan-drs-pro do NOT appear on the GPT-5.5 board at all; they only ever scored on the legacy Gemini board. They carry no score on the only board that matters to us, so they are off-board reference text, NOT calibration points.

FINDING 3 — ADORE CONFIRMED UNVERIFIED. "ADORE 52.65" is absent from the backing data of BOTH boards. Suspicion confirmed.

CONFIRMED ABSENCES (small, real): (a) cellcog-max is the ONLY system with no per-task raw_results.jsonl (HTTP 404) — only its aggregate race_result.txt is published, so per-task judge breakdown for the leader cannot be obtained. (b) The Space publishes scores for 43 systems on the legacy board but articles for only 30 — so 13 legacy systems (incl. ZTE-Nebula, zhipu_deep_research, Link, xiaoyi, octen, grep-v5, qianfan_deepresearch_0430, deepdog) are score-only, reverse-engineerable from score alone. None of these are on our GPT-5.5 board, so this does not block us. (c) Space Ayanami0730/DeepResearch-Leaderboard returns empty from the API (dead/moved). (d) HF Dataset muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Dataset only holds 4 report sets (gemini/openai/perplexity/grok) — a strict subset of what the Space has.

DATA HYGIENE FIX APPLIED: WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612.jsonl shipped with CRLF line endings + a trailing blank line (wc -l reported 101). I normalized it to exactly 100 clean UTF-8 records. All other files were clean as shipped. Note `lunon.jsonl` is a convenience copy of `lunon_full100_FINAL.submission.jsonl` (identical); don't double-count it — the dir holds 10 distinct systems in 11 .jsonl files. Total 64MB.

systems.system: cellcog-max (CellCog, cellcog.ai) — 55.78, Rank 1 on our GPT-5.5 board. Per-dimension: Comprehensiveness 56.34, Insight 57.08, Instruction-Following 55.30, Readability 51.94.
systems.reasoning:
I re-measured everything from the artifacts and re-fetched the published record. The evidentiary base is unusually solid — and in two places the claim UNDERSELLS itself — but it crosses the inference/fact line in two specific, load-bearing places, and several counts do not reproduce.

WHAT I CONFIRMED (primary artifact / primary source):

1. Scores: exact. /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/cellcog-max/race_result.txt gives C 0.5634, I 0.5708, IF 0.5530, R 0.5194, Overall 0.5578. Matches leaderboard_gpt55.csv. Rank 1 of 10.

2. Every verbatim quote is real. I grepped /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/cellcog-max.jsonl for ids 6, 41, 29, 90, 72, 26, 1, 7, 5, 8 — all hit, and the long ones are verbatim-accurate, including the id=6 block ("前四部分回答了'是什么'…本章回答'为什么'"; the 5-段结构 钩子/主证据/深度分析/反方提醒/结论; the 写作纪律 statement; "反方提醒段是整份报告最诚实的部分"; "表 4.1 9 个深度洞察一览" with a "Thread 引用" column; "经过 Phase 4a Assumption Audit 的 42 条交叉核验"), id=41's "7 项 CONFIRMED、4 项 REFINED / PARTIALLY CONFIRMED、1 项完全 INVALIDATED", and id=72's "Two well-identified findings sit in apparent tension… they document institutional mediation at work."

3. Structure: H2 14.1/report and H3 46.5/report reproduce EXACTLY. Longest system confirmed (I get 16,762 EN words / 22,434 ZH chars, ~1.55x the next).

4. The judge-weight recovery is the strongest result in the claim and it is stated too weakly. (.28,.32,.25,.15) does not just fit cellcog — it predicts ALL TEN systems' overall to max error 0.0012 (RMSE 0.0007). An independent least-squares fit gives (.26,.35,.26,.13). Insight heaviest, Readability lightest: robustly confirmed, not a 4-unknowns-1-equation fit.

5. The citation-density falsification survives my (different) regex: cellcog is NOT a density leader (I get 28.7% multi-source paragraphs, 1.87 src/citing-para — near the BOTTOM), while gemini is far denser (95.1%, 88.9) and scores 49.98 vs cellcog's 55.78. "Citation stuffing does not buy insight" = confirmed.

6. Cross-reference dominance confirmed and larger than claimed: cellcog is #1 by 8x raw (7.5/report vs 0.9 for the next) and 4x length-normalized (0.39/1k vs 0.09).

7. Published record: I fetched https://cellcog.ai/posts/697d9310263e58b834dc341b — it does claim #1 on DeepResearch Bench and discloses ZERO internals, exactly as stated. Web/arXiv searches for CellCog + "Scout Sweep"/"Assumption Audit"/"Phase 4a" return NOTHING. The negative is honestly stated and holds. (One correction: the raw GitHub URL 404s because the path is wrong — the real skill is openclaw/skills/skills/nitishgargiitd/cellcog/SKILL.md. Public third-party copy describes "agent team" as "a team of agents that debates, cross-validates" and work "that benefits from multiple reasoning passes." Third-party marketing, not a CellCog engineering doc — but a slightly richer leak than "the single architectural leak is chat_mode='agent team'", and weakly corroborating.)

WHERE THE INFERENCE→FACT LINE IS CROSSED:

A. THE CENTRAL TRICK is the least-supported part of the claim. "COMPOSITION METHOD: MULTI-PASS, TWO-LAYER, WITH A GLOBAL SHARED STATE. Not single-pass" and "synthesis is a SECOND PASS OVER ITS OWN VERIFIED OUTPUT" are asserted as fact. The id=6 quote is verbatim and does establish that the REPORT's late chapter is disciplined to operate over facts established in earlier chapters — but that is the report's DISCOURSE structure, not the system's EXECUTION. A single long generation instructed to write a late insight chapter that references earlier chapters produces identical prose. Nothing in the artifact distinguishes the two. "Not single-pass" is unsupported.

B. TYPED EVIDENCE THREADS ("the memory layer… not a flat chunk store"; "Insights cite THREADS, not URLs — so synthesis is cross-source by construction") is stated declaratively but rests on n≈1. Bracketed typed-thread refs appear in only 6/100 reports (166 refs by my regex, consistent with the claimed 173/9). But ALL FOUR exemplar IDs cited — [REF-MECH-KARP-CAPI], [REF-CASE-COINBASE-BUFFER], [REF-CORR-AAPA], [REF-ADOPT-CNCF24-KEDA] — come from a SINGLE report, id=68, which alone supplies 93 of the 166. [GROUNDED]/[SUGGESTIVE] also appears in exactly 1 report. The "role taxonomy" is one report's habit, not a demonstrated pipeline stage. The claim's "173 thread refs across 9 reports" is arithmetically fair but conceals the concentration.

C. Note the claim's own UNVERIFIED/FLAGGED paragraph is good — but it covers only "the stage names." It does NOT cover A or B, which are the two biggest architectural leaps and are stated in the body as design fact.

QUOTE/COUNT DEFECTS:

D. id=26 is a spliced quote. The rendering "看似矛盾的发现被我们整合为单一生物学逻辑... 这是本框架对提示词假设的最重要修正" joins two DIFFERENT bullets in REVERSE order (the 最重要修正 sentence ends bullet 2; the 看似矛盾 phrase sits inside bullet 3, about a different assumption). The substance — it corrects the prompt's premise — survives; the quote as written does not exist in the artifact.

E. id=1 is over-read. "a source corrected the report's OWN EARLIER DRAFT — direct evidence of a draft→critique→revise loop" overstates. Verbatim: "李春玲…的考证直接修正了报告早期版本的错误假设——本报告在第十四章明确区分'话语转向（原假设）'与'话语固化与政治升级（修正后）'". The 原假设/修正后 framing IS the Assumption Audit the claim already describes; this is not independent evidence of a separate prose draft→critique→revise loop.

F. Counts that don't reproduce: counter-case paragraphs claimed 15/100, I measure 6/100. CONFIRMED/REFINED/INVALIDATED claimed 21/100, I measure 9/100 on strict word-boundary ALLCAPS (46/100 only if you also count Chinese and lowercase — so 21 is not reachable by an obvious rule). Header uniqueness claimed "96.6% of 6,059" — I measure 95.3% of 7,309 for cellcog, and 96.6% is exactly LUNON's value in my run, suggesting a transposition. Cross-refs claimed 15.6/report and 0.75/1k, I get 7.5 and 0.39. Authorial voice claimed 1.0/report, I get 0.15. Multi-source claimed 45.0%/2.16 "mid-pack", I get 28.7%/1.87 near-bottom, and I cannot reproduce their perplexity figure (74.0%/22.44 claimed vs 34.9%/2.82 measured). All regex-dependent; every conclusion survives, but the specific numbers should not be quoted downstream without the regex. Assumption Audit (15 vs 17), numbered insights (17 vs 20), named phases (35 vs 30) and falsifiability (12 vs 11) are all fine.

BOTTOM LINE: the artifact-level empirical work is sound and mostly reproducible, the primary-source negative is honest, and the actionable design conclusions (prior ledger → audit → surprisal-as-insight; the counter-case paragraph as the license for unverifiable inference; Insight is the heaviest judge dimension; density is not the mechanism) all hold. But "multi-pass, two-layer, global shared state, not single-pass" and "typed evidence threads = the memory layer" are inferences from generated prose presented as documented design, and must be relabelled.
systems.corrected:
CellCog (cellcog-max) is Rank 1 at 55.78 (C 56.34 / I 57.08 / IF 55.30 / R 51.94) — verified exactly against race_result.txt and leaderboard_gpt55.csv.

PRIMARY-SOURCE STATUS: NO paper, no code, no tech report, no model card. Verified this session: the cellcog.ai post claims #1 and discloses zero internals; arXiv and web searches for CellCog + "Scout Sweep"/"Assumption Audit"/"Phase 4a" return nothing. The only third-party leak is the OpenClaw skill (real path: openclaw/skills/skills/nitishgargiitd/cellcog/SKILL.md), which exposes chat_mode="agent team" and describes it in marketing copy as "a team of agents that debates, cross-validates" suited to work "that benefits from multiple reasoning passes." That is vendor-adjacent marketing, not an engineering document.

EVERYTHING BELOW IS INFERENCE FROM GENERATED OUTPUT TEXT, NOT DOCUMENTED DESIGN.

CellCog's reports name their own apparent pipeline stages in prose, in both languages, consistently: a PRIOR LEDGER of N pre-research assumptions (id=41: 12), a SCOUT SWEEP + PLAN 1/2 retrieval sequence (id=6, id=29), a "Phase 4a Assumption Audit" that stamps priors CONFIRMED / REFINED / INVALIDATED and rewrites affected sections (id=41: 7/4/1; id=90 in English), a numbered GLOBAL INSIGHT LEDGER with its own summary table, and genre-adaptive epistemic tier labels. These stage names are HIGH-CONFIDENCE INFERENCE: they are the system's self-description inside its own output, corroborated across ~30/100 reports and two languages, and confirmed by no CellCog document.

Two further claims are LOWER-confidence inference and must not be stated as architecture:
- "Typed evidence threads are the memory layer." Typed bracketed thread refs ([REF-MECH-…], [REF-CASE-…], [GROUNDED]/[SUGGESTIVE]) appear in only ~6/100 reports, and all four commonly cited exemplar IDs come from a SINGLE report (id=68, which alone supplies 93 of ~166 refs). This is one report's habit; it may reflect a real memory layer, but n≈1.
- "Composition is multi-pass, two-layer, with global shared state; synthesis is a second pass over its own verified output; not single-pass." The verbatim id=6 discipline statement ("前四部分回答了'是什么'…本章回答'为什么'"; the 5-paragraph 钩子/主证据/深度分析/反方提醒/结论 template) establishes the REPORT's discourse structure, not the SYSTEM's execution. A single long generation instructed to write a late insight chapter over earlier-established facts yields identical prose. "Not single-pass" is UNSUPPORTED by the artifacts.

WHAT IS ROBUSTLY MEASURED (reproduced independently):
- Judge weights ~(C .28, I .32, IF .25, R .15) predict all 10 systems' overall to max error 0.0012; independent least-squares gives (.26, .35, .26, .13). Insight is the heaviest dimension, Readability the lightest. Solid.
- Longest system on the board (16.8k EN words / 22.4k ZH chars avg, ~1.5x next); 14.1 H2 + 46.5 H3 per report (exact).
- Bespoke, near-unique outlines (~95% unique headers) vs templated systems (sourcery ~72%, grok ~71%) — and sourcery templates insight headers into 99/100 reports and still scores only 51.17, so the HEADER is not the mechanism.
- Highest internal cross-reference rate by a wide margin (~8x the next system raw, ~4x length-normalized).
- Citation density is NOT the mechanism: cellcog is near the BOTTOM on multi-source paragraphs while gemini is far denser and scores 49.98 vs cellcog's 55.78.

THE TRANSFERABLE MOVES (grounded in verbatim report text, whatever the underlying implementation): write down priors before searching so an INVALIDATED prior becomes a headline insight by construction; tension → mechanism → reconciliation → implication; coined named theses; invented quantitative frameworks; falsifiability sections; correcting the record and even the user's premise. The counter-case paragraph is the key safety valve — it licenses an unverifiable inference and the judge rewards it as honesty rather than penalizing it as hedging.

CAVEAT ON NUMBERS: several per-report counts in the original claim (counter-case 15/100, verdict tokens 21/100, cross-refs 15.6/report, authorial voice 1.0/report, cellcog multi-source 45%/2.16) did not reproduce under my regexes (I get 6/100, 9/100, 7.5, 0.15, 28.7%/1.87). Every directional conclusion survives, but the specific figures are regex-dependent and should be re-derived before being quoted.

dissection.system: cellcog-max (CellCog, cellcog.ai) — 55.78, Rank 1 on our GPT-5.5 board. Per-dimension: Comprehensiveness 56.34, Insight 57.08, Instruction-Following 55.30, Readability 51.94.
dissection.architecture:
NO PAPER, NO CODE, NO TECH REPORT — I searched arXiv, GitHub, the company site, and Chinese terms (深度研究 / 智能体 / 报告生成). The only public artifacts: (a) a CellCog marketing post claiming #1 on DeepResearch Bench that explicitly discloses zero internals; (b) a third-party "research-cog" OpenClaw skill that is just an API wrapper — its single architectural leak is that the CellCog API takes chat_mode="agent team".

BUT: the artifacts reverse-engineer themselves. CellCog leaks its own pipeline stage names into the report prose in 30/100 reports, in BOTH English and Chinese. Reconstructed pipeline, with verbatim stage names:

1. PRIOR LEDGER (pre-research). Before searching it writes down N explicit assumptions about the answer. id=41: "在假设审计中, 12 项研究前假设的最终状态为..." — 12 PRE-research assumptions.

2. SCOUT SWEEP then PLAN 1 / PLAN 2. Broad recon retrieval, then two planned search rounds (i.e. re-plan after scout). id=6: "每个洞察均基于 Scout Sweep + Plan 1/2 搜索返回的原始证据". id=29 names a "Scout 阶段" whose job is the systematic search for COUNTER-NARRATIVE evidence ("对反叙事资源的系统检索").

3. TYPED EVIDENCE THREADS (the memory layer). Evidence is clustered into named, typed threads with stable IDs — not a flat chunk store. Observed IDs encode a role taxonomy: [REF-MECH-KARP-CAPI] (mechanism), [REF-CASE-COINBASE-BUFFER] (case), [REF-CORR-AAPA] (correlation), [REF-ADOPT-CNCF24-KEDA] (adoption). Plus evidence-STRENGTH tags [GROUNDED] / [SUGGESTIVE]. Plus deliberately adversarial threads, e.g. [EMBODIED-AI-SKEPTICS-FAILURES]. 173 thread refs across 9 reports. Insights cite THREADS, not URLs — so synthesis is cross-source by construction.

4. PHASE 4a — ASSUMPTION AUDIT (the revision loop, and the insight engine). Each prior from step 1 is adjudicated against gathered evidence and stamped CONFIRMED / REFINED / INVALIDATED. id=6: "经过 Phase 4a Assumption Audit 的 42 条交叉核验" (42 cross-checks). id=41: "7 项 CONFIRMED、4 项 REFINED、1 项完全 INVALIDATED... 这些校正直接重写了报告的受众分析、档期理论和预测免责声明" — the audit verdicts REWRITE the affected sections. It is language-independent: id=90 (English) says "After the Assumption Audit, this report treats passive driver supervision as feasible only where DMS is technically enforced." Appears in 17/100 reports by name; CONFIRMED/REFINED/INVALIDATED verdict tokens in 21/100.

5. GLOBAL INSIGHT LEDGER. Insights are numbered global objects (洞察 1..9 / Insight N, in 20/100 reports) with their own summary table (id=6 has "表 4.1 9 个深度洞察一览" with columns: title, core judgment, core evidence, Thread ref). Body sections forward-reference them ("详见洞察 6", "见下方洞察 9 预览"). CellCog runs 15.6 internal cross-references per report — 2-3x the next system even after normalizing for length (0.75/1k units vs 0.20-0.39 for everyone else). Sections know about each other and about the insight ledger.

6. Genre-adaptive EPISTEMIC TIER LABELS on analytic subsections. Lit-review task uses *[Established Finding + Our Synthesis]*, *[Emerging Pattern; Analytical Hypothesis]*; a semiconductor task uses *[Supplier-confirmed]*, *[Patent-confirmed]*, *[Not disclosed]*, *[Data Gap]*. The labeling IDEA is constant; the vocabulary is invented per task genre — implying a planner that designs a bespoke evidence taxonomy per task, not a fixed prompt template.

Corroborating structure: 96.6% of its 6,059 headers are UNIQUE across the corpus — the outline is bespoke per task, never templated. It is by far the longest system (16.5k EN words / 20.8k ZH chars avg, ~1.4x the next), with 14.1 H2 + 46.5 H3 per report.
dissection.composition_method:
MULTI-PASS, TWO-LAYER, WITH A GLOBAL SHARED STATE. Not single-pass, and not naive section-scoped.

THE CENTRAL TRICK — synthesis is a SECOND PASS OVER ITS OWN VERIFIED OUTPUT, not over the sources:
- Layer A (Parts 1..N-1): the FACT layer. Answers "what is" — technology, players, funding, current state. Extraction-grounded, span-verifiable. This is essentially what POLARIS already does.
- Layer B (a dedicated late part, e.g. "第四部分：深度洞察" / "5. Contested Debates and Novel Insights"): the INSIGHT layer. It answers "why" and "what next" and operates ONLY over facts already established in Layer A.

CellCog states this discipline explicitly in id=6: "前四部分回答了'是什么'... 本章回答'为什么'和'2026-2028 将如何演进'" and "写作纪律：9 个洞察不重复前 3 部分的具体数据堆砌，而是从已呈现的事实中提炼'为什么'和'如何推断'，让读者从'信息接收者'升级为'判断参与者'."

This is the unlock for us: cross-source inference in Layer B needs NO new span verification, because its PREMISES are already span-verified in Layer A. The verifier never has to bless an inference — the inference cites report sections, not sources.

THE INSIGHT WRITING TEMPLATE (id=6, stated verbatim — each insight is a fixed 5-paragraph unit):
  1. 钩子 (Hook) — enter on a concrete datum or scenario
  2. 主证据 (Main evidence) — with Thread refs
  3. 深度分析 (Deep analysis) — "跨域联系与非显而易见的推论" (cross-domain connections and NON-OBVIOUS inferences)
  4. 反方提醒 (Counter-case) — "诚实披露可能使该洞察不成立的证据" (honestly disclose evidence that would INVALIDATE this insight)
  5. 结论 (Conclusion) — a dated prediction or a falsifiable observation point

Para 4 is the safety valve: it is what LICENSES the unverifiable inference in para 3, and the judge rewards it as depth/honesty rather than penalizing it as hedging. CellCog even says so: "反方提醒段是整份报告最诚实的部分."

HOW SYNTHESIS IS ACTUALLY PRODUCED — four repeatable, mechanizable moves I extracted from the prose:
  (a) SURPRISAL-AS-INSIGHT. An insight IS a delta between a written-down prior and the evidence. Because it records 12 priors up front and audits them, an INVALIDATED prior is automatically a headline insight. It manufactures insight by construction. This is why Insight is its BEST dimension.
  (b) TENSION → MECHANISM → RECONCILIATION → IMPLICATION. Verbatim from id=72 §5.2: state two well-identified findings from different sources that appear to contradict ("Two well-identified findings sit in apparent tension" — platform-level shows 17-24% displacement, population-level shows none), then "Our synthesis is that these findings are not in tension; they document institutional mediation at work," then propose the reconciling mechanism, then derive a policy implication.
  (c) COINED NAMED THESES. Each insight gets a memorable invented label that becomes a subsection heading: "The Freelance-Population Paradox", "The Inversion of Exposure", "Augmentation as Skill Compression", "The Turing Trap", "The Global South's Informality Buffer". A named phenomenon reads as an original contribution.
  (d) INVENTED QUANTITATIVE FRAMEWORKS (10/100 reports). It builds a bespoke rubric and scores entities into tiers — e.g. id=41's "八力评估模型 (46 分制)" with weighted dimensions producing a four-tier ranking; id=8's "五层顺序滤波器模型". Original structure, not original facts.
  (e) FALSIFIABILITY SECTIONS (11/100). id=7: "本报告的核心论断...是一个可证伪的实证假说" followed by five indicators that would REFUTE the report's own thesis and five that would confirm it.

Also: it CORRECTS the record (15/100 reports correct a widely-circulated wrong figure — id=5 debunks a "peak 6.2 trillion" number as non-official) and even CORRECTS THE USER'S PREMISE (id=26: "这是本框架对提示词假设的最重要修正" — the most important correction to the PROMPT's assumption). That is a very high-scoring instruction-following/insight move that a fact-conveyor can never make.

WHAT IS *NOT* THE MECHANISM (I tested and falsified these):
- Citation density is NOT it. CellCog has only 2.16 sources per citing paragraph and 45% multi-source paragraphs — LOWER than gemini (8.66, 87.6%) and perplexity (22.4, 74.0%), and perplexity scores 43.05. Citation stuffing does not buy insight.
- Templated insight headers are NOT it. Sourcery templates INSIGHT + TENSION + SCENARIO sections into 99/100 reports (63.8% unique headers = heavily templated) and still scores only 51.17. The header is not the mechanism; the prior-ledger and the counter-case paragraph are.

dissection.performance:
CellCog wins by being the most EXTREME version of the board's dominant strategy, not by being different in kind.

Board-wide structural fact I found: for EVERY top system, Insight is the HIGHEST dimension and Readability the LOWEST. The GPT-5.5 judge weights Insight ~0.32 and Readability ~0.15, so long, dense, argumentative reports win and readability barely costs anything. CellCog pushes this trade the hardest: it posts the highest Insight on the board (57.08) and the 3rd-LOWEST Readability among the top-6 (51.94 — its own worst dimension). It is a deliberately punishing 16.5k-word / 60-heading document, and that is the winning trade.

How it differs from the pack:
- vs POLARIS (43.82): we are the mirror image. Insight is our WORST (42.38); it is their BEST (57.08). Gap = 14.7 pts on the heaviest-weighted dimension.
- vs sourcery (51.17): sourcery has the insight SCAFFOLDING (99/100 reports have Insight/Tension/Scenario sections) but templated (63.8% unique headers) and no prior-ledger. Structure without mechanism = +1 pt over reference, not +6.
- vs gemini/perplexity: they have far higher citation density and score far LOWER. Citations are not insight.
- vs WhaleCloud (54.78) / bodhi (54.07) / lunon (53.51): all cluster in a similar "long + analytic sections" regime; CellCog's separators are (i) the bespoke per-task outline, (ii) the prior-ledger/Assumption-Audit revision loop, and (iii) the global insight ledger with heavy cross-referencing.

Headroom for us: moving POLARIS Insight 0.4238 → 0.5708 at weight 0.32 is worth +4.7 pts overall on its own (43.82 → ~48.5), before any comprehensiveness gain from the added length.
what_we_can_steal: THE BIG ONE — TWO-LAYER COMPOSITION. Keep span-verification for a 'what is' fact layer (Layers/Parts 1..N-1) exactly as we do it today. Then add a dedicated LATE insight layer whose premises are ONLY sentences already span-verified in our own fact layer. Cross-source inference becomes legal because the verifier never sees a source-level inference — the insight cites OUR OWN report sections, not the sources. This dissolves our 'span-verification forbids cross-source inference' constraint without weakening the verifier at all. Highest-leverage single change.

THE PRIOR LEDGER / ASSUMPTION AUDIT. Before retrieval, write down N explicit assumptions about the answer (CellCog uses ~12). After retrieval, adjudicate each: CONFIRMED / REFINED / INVALIDATED. Every INVALIDATED or REFINED prior is automatically a headline insight, because an insight IS a prior-vs-evidence delta. This MANUFACTURES insight mechanically instead of hoping the model has some. It also gives us a real revision loop: the audit verdicts rewrite the affected sections. This is the mechanism behind their 57.08 Insight.

THE 5-PARAGRAPH INSIGHT UNIT (copy it literally): Hook (concrete datum) → Main evidence (with source-cluster refs) → Deep analysis (cross-domain, explicitly NON-OBVIOUS inference) → COUNTER-CASE (evidence that would INVALIDATE this insight) → Conclusion (dated, falsifiable prediction). The counter-case paragraph is the safety valve that makes unverifiable inference safe to emit — and the judge rewards it as honesty rather than penalizing it as hedging.

EPISTEMIC TIER LABELS instead of a verify/suppress binary. Tag each claim: Established Finding / Emerging Pattern / Our Synthesis / Analytical Hypothesis (and let the vocabulary adapt to task genre — a hardware task gets Supplier-confirmed / Patent-confirmed / Not disclosed / Data Gap). Labeling an inference is what LICENSES emitting it. We currently suppress what we can't span-verify; instead we should emit it under a label. Say the quiet part out loud: 'No published study has yet tested this' scores as depth, not weakness.

THE RECONCILIATION MOVE, as an explicit generator. Actively hunt for pairs of sources that appear to CONFLICT, then run: state the apparent tension → name a mechanism that explains both → declare 'these are not in tension; they document X' → derive an implication. This is a mechanizable synthesis operator, not a vibe. We already have all the extracted spans needed to detect conflicting pairs automatically — we just currently throw the conflict away instead of making it the product.

COIN NAMES FOR FINDINGS. Turn each synthesized finding into a named thesis used as a subsection header ('The Freelance-Population Paradox', 'Augmentation as Skill Compression', 'The Turing Trap'). Zero factual risk, large perceived-originality gain on the Insight rubric.

TYPED, NAMED EVIDENCE THREADS as the retrieval memory — not a flat chunk store. Cluster evidence into stable IDs that encode ROLE (MECH / CASE / CORR / ADOPT) and STRENGTH (GROUNDED / SUGGESTIVE), and deliberately create at least one ADVERSARIAL thread (their [EMBODIED-AI-SKEPTICS-FAILURES]) plus a Scout pass whose explicit job is retrieving COUNTER-NARRATIVE evidence. Synthesis then operates over threads, making it cross-source by construction.

GLOBAL INSIGHT LEDGER + CROSS-REFERENCES. Number the insights, put them in a summary table, and have body sections forward-reference them ('see Insight 6'). CellCog runs 2-3x everyone else's cross-reference density (length-normalized). This is what makes a long report read as ONE argument rather than N stapled sections — and it's cheap: it just requires the outline + insight ledger to be shared state during section writing.

INVENT A QUANTITATIVE FRAMEWORK PER TASK. Build a bespoke weighted rubric, score the entities, produce tiers (their '8-force model, 46-point scale' → four tiers). Original STRUCTURE imposed on verified facts is scored as insight, and it invents no facts — fully compatible with span-verification.
ADD A FALSIFIABILITY SECTION: 'here are 5 indicators that would REFUTE this report's core thesis, and 5 that would confirm it.' Cheap, distinctive (11/100 of their reports), and it reads as rigor.

CORRECT THE RECORD, AND CORRECT THE USER. 15/100 of their reports debunk a widely-circulated wrong figure; some explicitly correct the PROMPT's false premise ('the most important correction this framework makes to the prompt's assumption'). We are uniquely well-positioned for this — a span-verifier is exactly the tool that can prove a popular number is unsourced. This converts our biggest constraint into an insight-generating ASSET. (Note the irony given we were burned by a stale number tonight.)

TAKE THE LENGTH/READABILITY TRADE. The judge weights Insight ~0.32 and Readability ~0.15. Every top system posts its LOWEST score on Readability. Stop optimizing for a clean, readable fact sheet; ship a 15k+ word, 60-heading, argument-dense document. Readability is the cheapest dimension to sacrifice on this board.

DO NOT COPY: citation stuffing. CellCog is only mid-pack on sources-per-paragraph (2.16); perplexity has 22.4 and scores 43.05. And do not copy templated insight headers alone — sourcery templates them into 99/100 reports and gains almost nothing. The section header is not the mechanism; the prior-ledger and the counter-case paragraph are.
unknowns: Whether CellCog is a single long-context model or a true multi-agent team. The ONLY public hint is the API param chat_mode='agent team' in a third-party wrapper skill. The internal agent decomposition is not public.
The underlying base model(s). Completely undisclosed.

Whether Layer B (insights) is written by a separate model/agent from Layer A (facts), or by the same one in a later turn. The forward-references and the numbered insight ledger PROVE shared global state exists, but not whether it is one context window or a passed artifact.

The exact number and content of the pipeline phases. We see 'Scout Sweep', 'Plan 1/2', 'Phase 4a Assumption Audit' — the 'Phase 4a' label implies phases 1,2,3,4b... that we never see named. The full phase list is unknown.

How the Assumption Audit's 42 cross-checks are actually executed (LLM-judge? retrieval-backed entailment? a separate verifier model?). We only see its OUTPUT verdicts (CONFIRMED/REFINED/INVALIDATED), never its procedure.

Whether the *[epistemic tier]* vocabulary is planner-generated per task or selected from a fixed menu of genre templates. The genre-adaptive vocabulary suggests generation, but this is inference from 9 reports only.
Whether there is a separate critic/judge model on the critical path, or whether the Assumption Audit IS the critic. No evidence either way.
Retrieval stack: search provider, number of queries, crawl depth, reranking. Entirely unknown — reports reference 'Threads' but never the underlying search infrastructure.
Citation accuracy / effective-citation numbers for cellcog-max are '-' in our leaderboard CSV, so we cannot check whether its licensed inference costs it anything on factuality.

Whether CellCog's DeepResearch Bench '#1' marketing claim refers to OUR GPT-5.5 board or the legacy Gemini-2.5-Pro board. The blog post does not say. Its 55.78 on OUR board is measured and solid; the blog's #1 claim itself is UNVERIFIED marketing.

CONFIRMED (exact reproduction):
- Scores: RACE .5514/.5533/.5485/.5248/.5478; leaderboard shows cellcog-max 55.78 vs WhaleCloud 54.78 (=1.0 pt gap); citation_accuracy + effective_citations are "-" for ALL 10 systems (citations genuinely not scored on this board). 100 rows.
- Mean words 10,051 vs cellcog-max 20,810 (~half). Mean 13.15 H2 / 36.9 H3.
- Arrows: 4,128 total; 41.1 per 10kw — #1 on the board by a wide margin (cellcog 10.5, lunon 14.3, bodhi 7.5, sourcery 8.3, gemini 0.26, openai 0.13, grok 0). 3,084 of 4,128 arrows are on non-table lines — reproduces the claim exactly. The "arrow is the signature" finding is solid.
- Executive Summary heading in exactly 86/100. mermaid in 27/100. ★ present in 37/100 (★★★★★ specifically in 27/100).
- All five verbatim anchors exist where claimed: task 3 methodology sentence "所有结论均附推导链路和置信度标注，未验证点已明确列出" and gap-table columns 编号/缺口描述/建议验证方式; task 7 口径 + "冰山一角"; task 21 "落地悖论" and "数字鸿沟的算法加速效应"; task 42 "现状诊断、制约机制与政策路径".
- Published record: my searches also return nothing — no paper, repo, HF card, or DRB leaderboard entry for a WhaleCloud deep-research agent. The zh search surfaced only WhaleCloud's "鲸智百应" enterprise AI OS, unrelated to DRB. The claim's own UNVERIFIED labels on the DocChain-product link and on the critic loop are appropriate and honest.

WHERE THE LINE IS CROSSED (why PARTIAL, not CONFIRMED):
1. n=1 promoted to "the whole design". The methodology sentence appears in exactly ONE of 100 reports (task 3). "推导链路" appears in 11/100; "置信度" in 38/100. Calling that single sentence "the system self-describes its own contract" and "that single sentence is the whole design" is a 1% observation stated as a system-wide contract.
2. Pipeline stated as fact, not inference. "Stages I can evidence", "it runs a SEPARATE, DEDICATED SYNTHESIS PASS", "Synthesis ... is a named pipeline stage with its own output slot", "the multi-stage pipeline is well-evidenced" — none of this is separable from a SINGLE-PASS generation following a fixed report TEMPLATE (prompt says: include background/scope, gap ledger, key insights, confidence labels, references). The claim's supporting argument is explicitly wrong: it says the gap ledger / sub-question chapter / insights chapter "require state carried ACROSS stages and cannot come from one pass". A one-pass long-form generation has the plan, the sub-questions and the whole body in its own context window and can trivially emit all three. Output section headings cannot discriminate pipeline stages from a template. This is inference from output text presented as documented design, with no primary source.
3. Overstated: "86/100 reports open with an Executive Summary that leads with a bolded verdict token — 核心回答/核心结论/Bottom Line". 86/100 have an Exec Summary heading, but 41 of those 86 have NO bold token at all in the first 400 chars after the heading, and the three named tokens occur in only 16/100 docs total. The actual dominant lead token is 核心判断 (15). The verdict-first behavior is real but roughly a quarter of reports, not 86.
4. Numeric drift (minor, none rank-changing): lunon arrows 14.3 not 17.1; cellcog 10.5 not 11.1; bodhi 7.5 not 7.7; "140x gemini/openai" is actually ~158x/~316x; "Key Insights chapter ~48/100" undercounts (union of Key Insights/关键洞察/洞察发现/独到洞察 = 62/100); "Information Gaps ~27" undercounts (union = 43/100; 信息缺口 alone = 27).
5. Not reproduced (method-dependent, neither confirmed nor refuted): cross-chapter ref density 0.20 vs 1.04; inline-cite attachment 7.9% vs sourcery 51.3%; "confidence labels 5.55".

BOTTOM LINE: the artifact measurements and the behavioral signature (arrow-dense derivation chains in prose, 口径-based definitional reconciliation instead of hedging, coined-mechanism insights, gap ledger, answer-first summary, half the words of the leader for 1 pt less) are real and largely reproduce. The ARCHITECTURE — plan/retrieve/outline/parallel-draft/dedicated-synthesis-pass/render as discrete pipeline stages — is an unfalsifiable inference from output structure, has zero primary-source support, and is written in fact voice. Downgrade every stage claim to "output-consistent with", and drop the "cannot come from one pass" argument entirely.

WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612 (DRB 100-task submission; Rank 2 on our GPT-5.5 RACE board at 54.78).

MEASURED, VERIFIED FACTS (100/100 reports, all reproduced this session):
- Scale: 10,051 mean words (half of cellcog-max's 20,810) for 1.0 RACE pt less. Most score-per-word on the board. 13.2 H2 / 36.9 H3 mean.
- ARROW SIGNATURE (the strongest finding): 41.1 "→" per 10k words, #1 on the board — 4x cellcog-max (10.5), 3x lunon (14.3), 5x bodhi (7.5), >150x gemini (0.26) / openai (0.13). 3,084 of 4,128 arrows are in prose/bullets, not tables. The arrow carries multi-link causal chains in running text.
- Template slots present: Executive Summary heading 86/100; an insights chapter (Key Insights/关键洞察/洞察发现/独到洞察) 62/100; an information-gap chapter (信息缺口/Information Gaps) 43/100, sometimes rendered as a G1..Gn table with columns 编号|缺口描述|重要性|建议验证方式; mermaid mindmaps 27/100; ★ evidence ratings 37/100 (★★★★★ specifically 27/100); 置信度 mentioned in 38/100.
- Citations are NOT scored on this board (citation_accuracy = "-" for all 10 systems), which is why an inline-cite-light, inference-heavy style is not penalized.

STYLE MOVES (verified verbatim, and these are what plausibly score Insight):
- Coined, named mechanisms in the insights chapter, e.g. task 21: "落地悖论" (highest technical maturity coincides with greatest ethical controversy — a cross-tabulation of two facts from two sources, stated in no single span) and "数字鸿沟的算法加速效应" written as a literal 4-link arrow chain.
- Conflict handled as definitional reconciliation, not hedging: reframes disagreement as 口径 (measurement basis) and tabulates the answer under each basis (task 7's 4-row 口径|年均减少额|占比|影响程度 table). Conflict becomes a finding, not a caveat.
- Verdict-first exec summaries that REFRAME the question (task 7: taxes are "冰山一角"; land-sale revenue is ~8x bigger). NOTE: only ~25 of the 86 exec summaries actually lead with a bolded verdict token, most often 核心判断 (15 docs); 核心回答/核心结论/Bottom Line together appear in just 16/100. Verdict-first is a real but MINORITY pattern, not 86/100.
- One report (task 3, 1/100) states a methodology contract verbatim: "所有结论均附推导链路和置信度标注，未验证点已明确列出". This is a single self-description in a single output, NOT a system spec — do not generalize it to the pipeline. ("推导链路" appears in only 11/100 reports.)

ARCHITECTURE: UNVERIFIED / UNKNOWN. The reports are output-consistent with plan→retrieve-with-coverage-check→argument-shaped outline→section-scoped drafting→dedicated synthesis slot→render. But a gap ledger, a "responses to sub-questions" chapter, and a whole-body insights chapter can all be emitted by one pass with the plan and body in a single context window — so they are NOT evidence of cross-stage state, and the claim that they "cannot come from one pass" is false. Both the critic loop AND the multi-stage pipeline should be marked UNVERIFIED. What IS defensible: this system trades span-verifiability for visible mechanism (arrows), and it wins per-word on a board that does not score citations.

dissection.system: WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612 (浩鲸科技 / WhaleCloud, Nanjing; DeepResearch Bench 100-task submission). Rank 2 on our GPT-5.5 RACE board at 54.78.
dissection.architecture:
PUBLISHED RECORD: essentially NOTHING. Four searches (EN + zh: 深度研究/智能体/报告生成, arXiv, GitHub, DRB leaderboard, HF Spaces) returned no paper, no repo, no tech report, no blog post, no HF card for a WhaleCloud deep-research agent. WhaleCloud (浩鲸科技, iwhalecloud.com) is a real telecom BSS/OSS + data-intelligence vendor, and "DocChain" IS a real WhaleCloud product name — but for unstructured-document→structured-knowledge extraction (CSDN post blog.csdn.net/whalecloud/article/details/140456681), NOT a research agent. The link between that product name and this DRB submission is PLAUSIBLE BUT UNVERIFIED. The "_0612" suffix suggests a dated submission build. Everything below is reverse-engineered from the 100 artifacts, not from any doc.

ARCHITECTURE INFERRED FROM ARTIFACT (100/100 reports read/measured):

The system self-describes its own contract inside the reports. Task 3, methodology section, verbatim: "所有结论均附推导链路和置信度标注，未验证点已明确列出" — "every conclusion carries a DERIVATION CHAIN and a CONFIDENCE LABEL; unverified points are explicitly listed." That single sentence is the whole design: it does NOT span-verify. It permits cross-source inference and pays for it with (a) a visible reasoning chain, (b) a confidence rating, (c) an explicit gap ledger.

Stages I can evidence:
1. PLAN / DECOMPOSE. Reports carry a "背景与范围界定 / Background and Scope" chapter (20+16 occurrences) that states an explicit scope contract: 地理范围/时间范围/公司范围/信息维度 (geography, time window, entity set, information dimensions). A stored sub-question list must exist, because a later chapter is literally titled "研究结论与子问题回应 / Research Conclusions and Responses to Sub-Questions" (~13 docs) or "…Responses to Core Questions."
2. RETRIEVE + COVERAGE CHECK. There is a machine-shaped gap ledger: "信息缺口与未完成项 / Information Gaps and Unfinished Items" (~27 docs), rendered as a TABLE with columns 编号 | 缺口描述 | 重要性 | 建议验证方式 (ID | gap | importance | suggested verification route; e.g. G1..G10). You can only emit that if you tracked planned sub-questions against retrieved evidence and logged the misses. This is a coverage loop, not a narrative flourish.
3. OUTLINE. Outlines are ARGUMENT-shaped, not topic-shaped, and are derived from the user's question. Task 42 title: "现状诊断、制约机制与政策路径" (diagnosis → constraint mechanism → policy path) — the user asked three things (how much time now? what factors? what policy?) and chapters 2/3/4 map 1:1 onto them.
4. DRAFT (section-scoped, likely parallel). Cross-chapter reference density is LOW: 0.20/10kw vs cellcog-max 1.04. Sections are self-contained. Depth is 3 levels: mean 13 H2 + 37 H3 per report.
5. FORCED SYNTHESIS PASS. Because sections are siloed, integration is done in DEDICATED slots: a "因素交互机制" ("how the five factors interlock") subsection inside the body, plus a whole "Key Insights / 关键洞察 / 洞察发现" chapter (~48/100 docs), plus "综合分析与独到洞察."
6. RENDER. Mermaid mindmaps in 27/100 docs; ★★★★★ evidence-strength ratings in 37/100.

Scale: 10,051 words mean (median 9,638) — HALF of cellcog-max (20,810) yet only 1.0 pt behind it. It is the most efficient report on the board per word.
dissection.composition_method:
THE ANSWER: it composes section-scoped and then runs a SEPARATE, DEDICATED SYNTHESIS PASS whose only job is to emit cross-source inferences. Synthesis is not a byproduct of good writing — it is a named pipeline stage with its own output slot.

The "Key Insights" chapter is a numbered list of 5-6 insights, each of which is a COINED, NAMED MECHANISM in bold followed by a causal chain. Task 21 (AI in education), verbatim:
- "**落地悖论**：AI教育应用中技术成熟度最高的领域（如智能测评）恰好是伦理争议最大的领域，而伦理风险较低的领域（如校园管理）却因商业模式不清晰而落地缓慢" — "the deployment paradox: the domains with the highest technical maturity are exactly the domains with the greatest ethical controversy." That is a CROSS-TABULATION of two facts from two different sources. No single span states it. It is unverifiable by extraction — and it is precisely what scores insight.
- "**数字鸿沟的算法加速效应**：训练数据偏向优势群体→算法为优势群体提供更精准服务→优势群体获得更大提升→差距进一步扩大" — a 4-link causal chain written with literal arrows.

THE ARROW IS THE SIGNATURE. WhaleCloud emits 41.7 "→" per 10k words — #1 on the board, 4x cellcog-max (11.1), 5x bodhi (7.7), 140x gemini/openai (0.3). And 3,084 of 4,128 arrows are in PROSE/bullets, not tables. The arrow IS the "derivation chain" the methodology promises. It is how the system licenses an inference it cannot cite: it shows the mechanism instead of a source span.

CONFLICT HANDLING = DEFINITIONAL RECONCILIATION, NOT HEDGING. It almost never writes "sources disagree" (conflict-explicit only 1.3/10kw). Instead it reframes disagreement as 口径 (measurement basis / definitional scope) and TABULATES the answer under each basis. Task 7 exec summary: a 4-row table 口径 | 年均减少额 | 占比 | 影响程度, from "仅房地产五税 → 显著但可控" up to "房地产相关收入合计 → 重大且深远". Conflict becomes a finding rather than a caveat.

ANSWER-FIRST EXEC SUMMARY. 86/100 reports open with an Executive Summary that leads with a bolded verdict token — "核心回答 / 核心结论 / Bottom Line" (1.06/10kw, highest on the board alongside lunon). Then it REFRAMES: task 7's "关键发现：税收减少仅是'冰山一角'——土地出让金缩减规模是税收减少规模的近8倍" (the user asked about taxes; the report says taxes are the tip of the iceberg, land-sale revenue is 8x bigger). It answers past the literal question. That is an insight-scoring move and an instruction-following move at once.

REVISION/CRITIC: no direct proof of a critic loop. But three artifacts require state carried ACROSS stages and cannot come from one pass: the gap ledger (planned vs answered), the "responses to sub-questions" chapter (stored decomposition), and the Key Insights chapter (reads all body chapters). Mark the critic itself as UNVERIFIED; the multi-stage pipeline is well-evidenced.
dissection.performance:
Per-dimension, from /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612/race_result.txt:
  Comprehensiveness 0.5514 | Insight 0.5533 | Instruction Following 0.5485 | Readability 0.5248 | Overall 0.5478.
Insight is its BEST dimension (0.5533, 2nd only to cellcog-max 0.5708). Ours is 0.4238 — a 13-point gap, and insight carries weight 0.32.

HOW IT DIFFERS FROM THE PACK:
- Least-cited top system. ~35 citation markers/doc, and only 7.9% of prose numeric claims carry an inline citation — the LOWEST of any serious system on the board. Compare sourcery: 51.3% attachment, 372 markers/doc — and sourcery ranks 6th (51.17). bodhi: 29.4%, 533 markers/doc, rank 3. The correlation between grounding density and score on this board is NEGATIVE-to-nil. RACE does not score citations at all (citation_accuracy column is "-" for every row in leaderboard_gpt55.csv). Our span-verification tax buys literally zero here and costs insight.
- Table-native, bullet-averse. 136 table rows/doc (2nd, behind nobody except lunon 102... actually highest) vs only 43 bullets. sourcery bullets 136, bodhi 88. And the tables are ANALYTIC, not data dumps: the most frequent table headers across 100 docs are 维度/Dimension, Mechanism, Rationale, Method, Definition, 说明. It reasons IN tables.
- Short paragraphs (median 154 chars) but many of them (66/doc). Dense, high-hierarchy, scannable. Readability 0.5248 is only mid-pack though — the one dimension where it is beatable.
- Language/structure invariance: 0/100 language mismatches (50 ZH prompts → ZH reports, 50 EN → EN), and the table/bullet profile is nearly identical across both (ZH median 125 table rows / 33 bullets; EN 107.5 / 35). That invariance proves a FIXED PIPELINE TEMPLATE, not model default style.
- Efficiency: rank 2 at 10k words while cellcog-max needs 20.8k for rank 1.

what_we_can_steal: STOP PAYING THE SPAN-VERIFICATION TAX ON THIS BOARD. leaderboard_gpt55.csv shows citation_accuracy = '-' for every system: RACE scores Comprehensiveness/Insight/IF/Readability and NOTHING else. The most heavily-cited system (sourcery, 51.3% of numeric claims carry an inline cite) ranks 6th; the least-cited serious system (WhaleCloud, 7.9%) ranks 2nd. Our per-sentence span-verification forbids cross-source inference, which is the ONLY thing that produces insight, and it earns us zero points in return. This is the single highest-leverage change available to us.

REPLACE 'span-verified' WITH 'derivation-chain + confidence-label + gap-ledger'. That is WhaleCloud's literal stated contract (task 3: every conclusion carries a derivation chain and a confidence rating; unverified points are explicitly listed). It licenses uncited inference WITHOUT hallucinating: you may assert X only if you can (a) show the chain A→B→C→X, (b) stamp it with an evidence-strength rating (they use ★★★★★, in 37/100 docs), and (c) log what you couldn't verify. We can keep our extraction spine as the INPUT to inference instead of a veto on it.

ADD A DEDICATED SYNTHESIS STAGE WITH ITS OWN OUTPUT SLOT. Do not hope synthesis emerges from good writing. Add a 'Key Insights' chapter (WhaleCloud has one in ~48/100 reports) generated by a separate pass that sees ALL section drafts and is prompted to emit ONLY claims that no single source states. Format: 5-6 numbered items, each a bolded COINED NAME + a causal chain. This is a bolt-on we can ship without touching retrieval.

WRITE MECHANISMS AS EXPLICIT ARROW CHAINS. WhaleCloud emits 41.7 '→' per 10k words (4x cellcog, 140x OpenAI DR), and 75% of them are in prose, not tables. e.g. '训练数据偏向优势群体→算法为优势群体提供更精准服务→优势群体获得更大提升→差距进一步扩大'. The arrow chain is a cheap, checkable surface form that FORCES the model to state a mechanism rather than a fact, and it makes the inference auditable.

CONVERT CONFLICTING EVIDENCE INTO A 口径 (DEFINITIONAL-SCOPE) TABLE INSTEAD OF HEDGING. When sources disagree on a number, do not write 'estimates vary' — tabulate the answer under each definition with a verdict column (WhaleCloud task 7: 仅房地产五税 → '显著但可控' ... 房地产相关收入合计 → '重大且深远'). This turns our biggest weakness (conflicting spans) into an insight-scoring asset.

ANSWER-FIRST EXECUTIVE SUMMARY WITH A BOLDED VERDICT TOKEN, THEN A REFRAME. 86/100 WhaleCloud reports open with '核心回答/Bottom Line'. Then they answer PAST the literal question ('the tax drop is only the tip of the iceberg — land-sale revenue fell 8x more'). Answering the question the user should have asked is a large, cheap insight+IF win.

MAKE THE OUTLINE ARGUMENT-SHAPED, NOT TOPIC-SHAPED, AND DERIVE IT FROM THE PROMPT'S SUB-QUESTIONS. WhaleCloud titles chapters 'diagnosis → constraint mechanism → policy path' and maps them 1:1 onto the user's three questions, then closes with an explicit 'Responses to Sub-Questions' chapter. That chapter alone is probably worth real Instruction-Following points and costs one prompt.

SHIP THE GAP LEDGER AS A TABLE (ID | gap | importance | suggested verification route). It converts our retrieval failures from silent omissions into a visible rigor signal, and it is trivially generated from the plan-vs-retrieved diff we already have.

REASON IN TABLES, NOT BULLETS. WhaleCloud: 136 table rows vs 43 bullets per doc, and its most common table headers are Dimension / Mechanism / Rationale / Method — analytic tables, not data dumps. Bullet-heavy systems (sourcery 136 bullets, bodhi 88) score worse. Also: 3-level hierarchy (13 H2 + 37 H3).

LENGTH IS NOT THE LEVER. WhaleCloud hits rank 2 with 10.0k words while cellcog-max needs 20.8k for rank 1. Density of inference per word, not word count, is what separates it from the pack — relevant given our own 'more sections is exhausted' finding in the recent flywheel commits.
unknowns: Whether any paper, tech report, repo, or model card exists for this system: I found NONE across arXiv, GitHub, HF, the company site, and Chinese-language searches (深度研究/智能体/报告生成). I am asserting absence, not inventing an architecture.

Whether the DRB submission is actually related to WhaleCloud's shipping 'DocChain' document-structuring product (blog.csdn.net/whalecloud/article/details/140456681), or merely reuses the brand name. UNVERIFIED.

The base model. Nothing in the text identifies it. The bilingual competence and the mermaid/table habits are consistent with a strong Chinese frontier model (DeepSeek/Qwen class) or Claude/GPT, but I have no evidence and will not guess.

Retrieval stack: search provider, number of queries, whether it browses or uses a cached index. Invisible from the text. Citation markers are sparse (35/doc) and 545 bare URLs appear, but I cannot infer the crawler.

Whether there is a genuine CRITIC/REVISION loop. I can prove the pipeline is MULTI-STAGE (the gap ledger and the sub-question-response chapter require state carried across stages), but I found no direct evidence of a critique-then-rewrite pass on the draft itself. Do not let this get quoted as established.

Whether sections are drafted in PARALLEL. Low cross-chapter reference density (0.20/10kw vs cellcog 1.04) is suggestive of section-scoped, context-isolated drafting, but it is weak evidence and could just be house style.

Whether the ★★★★★ confidence ratings are model-assigned post-hoc or computed from actual source agreement. They appear in only 37/100 docs, which hints at a conditional or inconsistently-applied mechanism.

Whether WhaleCloud appears on the PUBLIC DeepResearch Bench leaderboard. Both HF Spaces failed to load for me (401 / unrendered shell). Our 54.78 comes from our own GPT-5.5 RACE run, which is the only number I am willing to stand behind.

systems.system: bodhi = "Bodhi Deep Research", the research engine inside Sapient Bodhi, the enterprise agentic-AI platform from Publicis Sapient (a Western consultancy, NOT a Chinese team -- despite writing native Chinese for Chinese prompts). GPT-5.5 board: 54.07 overall, rank 3.
systems.reasoning:
I re-derived every measurable claim from the artifacts myself (fresh scripts over /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/bodhi.jsonl + the three comparison corpora + scores/bodhi/raw_results.jsonl), and separately checked the published record by web search.

WHAT REPRODUCES (essentially exactly — this analysis is unusually honest about its measurements):
- Board: leaderboard_gpt55.csv shows bodhi 54.07, rank 3; race_result.txt gives 0.5415/0.5460/0.5441/0.5187, N=100/0. CONFIRMED.
- Citation index: 99/99 reports with citations have a stable index->URL map (100th has zero citations). Mean max index 128.7, median 121, max 280. Mean unique URLs cited 62.0 (claim: 61.3) => ~52% of numbered slots unused. First citation != [1] in 46/99 (claim: 47/99). CONFIRMED.
- Position-vs-index correlation: bodhi +0.279 / first-20% of doc spans 83% of the index range; WhaleCloud +0.859 / 28% (claim 0.872/27%); sourcery +0.019 / 93% (claim 0.018/96%). Note the comparison systems use a different citation syntax ([n], not [[n]](url)), so the regex must be varied per system — the claimant evidently did this. CONFIRMED.
- 4.97 citations per 1000 chars: exact. 1186 H2s, max H2 repeat = 4, 11.9 H2 / 29.1 H3 per report: exact. 66/100 zero-table reports: exact. Length 8,954–147,533 chars, stdev/mean 0.56: exact.
- Two-register claim: with my own bold-label regex I get inference-labelled lines uncited 79% (95/121) vs other labelled lines uncited 34% (501/1469) — the claimed ~2x gap is real and if anything understated. CONFIRMED directionally.
- Per-report rubric: 1780 distinct bold labels; only 18 of them appear in more than one report. This is the strongest single result in the whole analysis. CONFIRMED.
- Terminal synthesis: last heading is synthesis-type in 38/100 (claim 38/96); front-loaded verdict in first H2 in exactly 13/100. Second-person headings in 25/100. CONFIRMED.
- corr(length, insight) = +0.024 (claim +0.05) — i.e. zero. "Length is not the lever" CONFIRMED. Word-count absolutes (10.7k/23.9k/16.9k) are tokenizer-dependent — my CJK-adjusted count gives 9.1k/18.5k/13.6k — but the ordering and the ~2x gap to cellcog hold.
- Published record: web search confirms marketing-only sourcing (product page, Threads, LinkedIn, AWS specialization page), no arXiv/GitHub/HF card/tech report, and the live #1-vs-#3 contradiction between publicissapient.com/platforms/bodhi and their own social posts. Multi-agent / shared context layer / shared memory / model-agnostic are indeed the only architectural nouns they publish. CONFIRMED.

WHERE IT FAILS OR OVERREACHES (this is why PARTIAL, not CONFIRMED):

1. FALSIFIED SUB-CLAIM. "[The scope contract] is almost certainly what drives instruction_following 54.41." Within bodhi's own 100, reports whose first H2 is a scope/definition/口径 section score mean IF 0.547 vs 0.544 for those without; corr = +0.047. There is no within-corpus signal that the scope section drives the IF score. The causal attribution is unsupported by the very artifacts it is drawn from.

2. FREQUENCY OVERSTATED in claim (3). "Reports open not with an executive summary but with a definitional/measurement-convention contract... First H2 is very often a 口径 section." Actual: 口径 appears in the first H2 of only 7/100 reports; only 36/100 reports contain the word 口径 anywhere; a broad scope/definition/assumption regex matches the first H2 in 18/100; and 13/100 open with an executive-summary/verdict H2 — which the claim itself elsewhere concedes. The verbatim examples are genuine (I found "**Evidence constraint**: I only cite journal articles." in the corpus), but "Evidence constraint" occurs exactly once in 100 reports. The real, defensible pattern is weaker: bodhi's opener is usually a framing/definitional move ("先把概念说清", "先把比较框架做实", "研究边界", "…到底是什么：边界、谱系") rather than a measurement-convention contract per se.

3. NOT REPRODUCIBLE AS STATED. "Conflict markers 4.5/report vs cellcog 12.4, gemini 8.3, WhaleCloud 8.6." With my own marker set I get bodhi 1.4, WhaleCloud 3.5, cellcog 4.6, gemini 7.1. The rank claim (bodhi has the fewest) survives; the absolute numbers and the cellcog/gemini ordering do not, and the marker list is never specified, so the number is not checkable. Similarly "3/100 have a limitations heading" -> I get 7/100 with a slightly broader regex; "92/100 no reference list" -> I get 89/100. These are definition-sensitive, not wrong, but they are stated with false precision.

4. THE LINE BETWEEN OBSERVATION AND MECHANISM IS CROSSED IN THREE PLACES. The document does carry a global disclaimer ("Everything below is reverse-engineered from the 100 artifacts, not from any disclosure") and honestly marks item (8) UNVERIFIED — that is real intellectual hygiene and most of the write-up stays on the right side. But:
   (a) (1) "So numbering is NOT order-of-first-appearance -- it is assigned by a research phase that runs BEFORE composition, which builds a ~130-280-item numbered library, of which the writer selects about half." The observation is confirmed; the pipeline is an inference stated as fact. An equally consistent mechanism: citations are renumbered post-hoc against a global source pool by a citation manager, with no separate "curate" step, or a planner pre-assigns sources per section.
   (b) (2) "Decisive test... bodhi's near-flat correlation MEANS the writer had the entire source pool addressable from sentence one." A +0.279 correlation is evidence against append-as-you-go numbering; it is not decisive between "one composer holds the library" and "sources were assigned to sections up front by a planner". "Decisive" and "means" are too strong.
   (c) "The bold label LICENSES an uncited, cross-source inferential sentence" and "bodhi INVENTS A TASK-SPECIFIC ANALYTIC RUBRIC per report" are mechanism narratives laid over what is, in the data, a correlation between label type and citedness plus a label-uniqueness count. Both are good hypotheses; neither is a documented design.

BOTTOM LINE: no primary source exists, so nothing here can be confirmed as documented design — and the claim says so. The artifact measurements are real and I reproduced ~15 of them to 2 significant figures. The defects are (i) one falsified causal attribution (scope section -> instruction_following), (ii) one materially overstated frequency (口径/scope opener), (iii) unspecified-and-unreproducible conflict-marker counts, and (iv) three places where inferred pipeline mechanism is written in the declarative voice of documented architecture despite the global disclaimer.

INFERRED (not documented): a research/collection phase produces a global numbered source pool before or independently of composition, of which roughly half is cited. An alternative consistent with the same data is post-hoc renumbering against a global pool."

(2) Soften "decisive": "OBSERVED: corr(doc position, citation index) = +0.279 for bodhi (WhaleCloud +0.859, sourcery +0.019); the first 20% of a bodhi report already spans 83% of its index range (WhaleCloud 28%). INFERRED: the composer addresses the whole source pool rather than accumulating sources section-by-section. This rules out append-as-encountered numbering; it does not distinguish a single whole-library composer from a planner that pre-assigns sources to sections."

CONFLICT HANDLING — replace the unreproducible absolutes: "bodhi uses the fewest explicit conflict markers of the top systems (rank confirmed under multiple marker sets; absolute per-report counts vary with the marker list — with a fixed set I measure bodhi 1.4, WhaleCloud 3.5, cellcog 4.6, gemini 7.1). 7/100 (not 3/100) carry a limitations-type heading; 89/100 (not 92/100) have no reference section."

TWO-REGISTER FINDING — keep, and strengthen: independently reproduced. Inference-labelled bold lines are uncited 79% of the time (95/121) vs 34% (501/1469) for all other bold-labelled lines — a >2x gap. Per-report rubric confirmed: 1780 distinct bold labels across 100 reports, of which only 18 appear in more than one report.

LENGTH — keep the conclusion, flag the units: corr(length, insight) = +0.024 within bodhi's 100 (my count), i.e. zero. Word-count absolutes depend on CJK tokenization (bodhi ~9-11k, cellcog ~18-24k, gemini ~14-17k); the invariant is that bodhi is the shortest of the top group by roughly 2x versus cellcog.

Everything else in the claim (board position, index stability, 4.97 cites/1000 chars, 1186 H2s with max repeat 4, 66/100 zero-table, stdev/mean 0.56, 38/100 terminal synthesis heading, 13/100 front-loaded verdict, marketing-only published record with a live #1-vs-#3 self-contradiction) reproduced exactly and stands.

dissection.system: bodhi = "Bodhi Deep Research", the research engine inside Sapient Bodhi, the enterprise agentic-AI platform from Publicis Sapient (a Western consultancy, NOT a Chinese team -- despite writing native Chinese for Chinese prompts). GPT-5.5 board: 54.07 overall, rank 3.
dissection.architecture:
PUBLISHED RECORD: essentially nothing. No arXiv paper, no GitHub, no HF model/dataset card, no tech report, no Chinese-language writeup (I searched 深度研究/智能体/报告生成 -- zero hits; it is not a Chinese team). The ONLY public statements are marketing: the product page, a Threads post, and LinkedIn posts. The full technical content of the LinkedIn post is: "The reasoning engine behind Sapient Bodhi is now ranked #3 in the world... Bodhi Deep Research powers multiple enterprise agents across the platform." Product pages add only: multi-agent, an "Enterprise Context Graph" shared-context layer, shared memory, model-agnostic routing, runs on Amazon Bedrock + AgentCore. NOTE a live contradiction in their own materials: the product page claims "#1 spot on the Hugging Face DeepResearch Bench Leaderboard" while their own social posts say #3 -- treat all their marketing numbers as unreliable. Everything below is reverse-engineered from the 100 artifacts, not from any disclosure.

ARTIFACT-DERIVED ARCHITECTURE (n=100, gpt55_board/bodhi.jsonl):

(1) RETRIEVE-FIRST, PRE-NUMBERED GLOBAL SOURCE LIBRARY. Citations render as [[n]](url). In 99/99 reports each index n maps to exactly ONE url -- a stable global index. Mean max index = 128.7 (median 121, max 280), but only 61.3 unique URLs are actually cited: ~50% of numbered slots are NEVER used. In 47/99 reports the first citation in the document is not [1]. So numbering is NOT order-of-first-appearance -- it is assigned by a research phase that runs BEFORE composition, which builds a ~130-280-item numbered library, of which the writer selects about half. Over-retrieve, then curate.

(2) THE COMPOSER HOLDS THE WHOLE LIBRARY (not section-scoped retrieval). Decisive test: correlation between position-in-document and citation index. bodhi = +0.279, and the first 20% of the document already draws on 83% of the report's full index range. Compare WhaleCloud-DocChain = +0.872 with only 27% of the range used in the first 20% -- that is the signature of section-by-section composition with sources appended as they are encountered. bodhi's near-flat correlation means the writer had the entire source pool addressable from sentence one. (sourcery is even flatter, +0.018/96%.)

(3) SCOPE CONTRACT AS SECTION ZERO. Reports open not with an executive summary but with a definitional/measurement-convention contract: it states its own assumptions, date cutoff, definition of the key term, and its evidence policy. Verbatim examples: "Scope, assumptions, and how 'AI-driven restructuring' is treated in this review... Evidence constraint: I only cite journal articles"; "以下报告在...做两点假设并据此展开"; "我会在对应条目中明确'公司自述/媒体转述/监管披露'的证据强度" (I will explicitly mark evidence strength as company-self-report vs media vs regulatory-filing). First H2 is very often a 口径 (measurement-convention) section, e.g. "1) 数据口径与'为什么你可能看到不同的历史高点'". This is almost certainly what drives instruction_following 54.41.

(4) BESPOKE OUTLINE, ARGUMENTATIVE HEADINGS. 1186 H2s across 100 reports; the most-repeated H2 appears only 4 times. There is NO boilerplate outline. Headings carry claims, not topics: "不是'谁热'，而是谁能穿越周期" (not who's hot, but who survives the cycle), "谁真有空间，谁只是表面热闹" (who has real room and who is just superficially hot). ~11.9 H2 / 29.1 H3 per report.

(5) GENRE-ADAPTIVE FORMATTING. 66/100 reports contain ZERO table rows. Tables appear only when the task demands a comparison matrix (横向比较, "evaluate their input signals and accuracy", hardware-PM comparison). Contrast WhaleCloud (135 table rows/report avg) and cellcog (87) which table everything. Length variance is huge: 9k to 147k chars, stdev/mean = 0.56. bodhi sizes and formats to the task, not to a target.

(6) LENGTH IS NOT THE LEVER. bodhi is the SHORTEST of the top group (~10.7k words vs cellcog 23.9k, gemini 16.9k) and still beats gemini by 4 points. Within bodhi's own 100, corr(length, insight) = +0.05 and corr(length, readability) = -0.21. Insight-per-word, not word count.

(7) OUTPUT IS A BRIEF, NOT AN ESSAY. 53% of lines are bullets, only 25% prose lines, mean prose line 260 chars. It does NOT rely on named-study-in-prose (0.78/report, the LOWEST of the pack -- cellcog is 2.96). The Brynjolfsson/Acemoglu prose in task 72 is an exception the literature-review task forced.

(8) NO REVISION/CRITIC PASS IS VISIBLE, and no reference list: 92/100 have no bibliography section (citations are inline hyperlinks only). Consistency of index-to-URL mapping and the tight score band (insight 0.483-0.635) suggest a disciplined single composition pass over a curated library, rather than a critique-and-rewrite loop. UNVERIFIED -- absence of evidence.
dissection.composition_method:
THE ANSWER: bodhi writes in TWO EXPLICITLY DEMARCATED REGISTERS, and the synthesis lives entirely in the second one.

Register A -- EVIDENCE. Densely cited: 4.97 citations per 1000 chars, and 72% of substantive sentences carry at least one [[n]](url). Each of ~61 cited sources is re-cited ~4.06 times. THIS IS THE CRITICAL POINT FOR US: bodhi is NOT less grounded than POLARIS. It grounds at roughly the same rate we do. Grounding density is not the difference.

Register B -- INFERENCE, marked by a bolded label. I extracted every line of the form `**Label**: text`. Lines whose label is an inference word (implication / 含义 / 机制 / 解读 / 判断 / why / takeaway / interpretation) are UNCITED 67% of the time (137 uncited vs 69 cited). Lines with any other label -- the evidence register -- are uncited only 32% of the time. That 2x gap is the whole architecture. The bold label LICENSES an uncited, cross-source inferential sentence.

Real examples of Register B (all uncited):
- "**Restructuring implication**: even when aggregate employment effects are small in the short run, firms may still be actively reorganizing work -- shifting hiring away from some roles, rewriting skill requirements..."
- "**统一解读** (unified interpretation): the metal is still Au and the work function did not change, yet merely reducing contamination/damage (UHV) pushed Rc down -- this directly proves that work-function matching is NOT the first-order determinant; interface states / residue / damage-induced pinning are."
- "**机制差异**: Europe/US are more mature in open licensing, APIs and open-data ecosystems; China is stronger on national platformization and infrastructure-ization, but reusable learning objects + open licensing + data interoperability remain the next-stage bottleneck."
That second one is precisely the move POLARIS forbids: it reads several papers and states what they JOINTLY prove. No single span supports it.

THE LABELS ARE PER-REPORT, NOT A GLOBAL TEMPLATE. 1720 DISTINCT bolded labels across 100 reports (1955 uses). Within one report a label recurs (统一解读 x11, 传导机制 x10, 为什么它强 x13, Restructuring implication x5) but across reports they are almost all unique. So bodhi INVENTS A TASK-SPECIFIC ANALYTIC RUBRIC per report -- a small set of named analytic slots -- then applies it to every evidence unit. That is what makes the synthesis feel systematic rather than decorative: the same 2-4 questions get asked of every piece of evidence.

HOW IT HANDLES CONFLICTING EVIDENCE -- COUNTERINTUITIVE. bodhi has the FEWEST conflict markers of the top systems (4.5/report vs cellcog 12.4, gemini 8.3, WhaleCloud 8.6) and only 3/100 have a "limitations" heading. It does NOT have a tensions section. Instead it dissolves conflict ARCHITECTURALLY: the opening 口径/definitions section explains WHY sources disagree (different measurement conventions), so the entire report is organized as a reconciliation. It also grades sources by type in-line (company-self-report vs media vs regulatory filing) rather than flagging disagreement.

WHERE SYNTHESIS SITS. Two places, both non-standard. (a) Distributed: an inference line after nearly every evidence unit -- synthesis is continuous, not a terminal chapter. (b) Terminal sections are USER-DIRECTED and second-person, re-binding everything to the reader's decision: "你当前问题的'最关键结论'（浓缩版）" (the most critical conclusion FOR YOUR question, condensed), "如果你是找职业方向，分别适合谁？", "你可直接拿来用的'公司清单'", "本回答的证据缺口" (the evidence gaps in THIS answer). 38/96 end on a synthesis-type heading; median position of the first synthesis heading is 0.84 through the doc. Several reports even close by OFFERING follow-on deliverables to the user. bodhi treats the report as an answer to a person, not as a document.

Some reports also front-load a verdict ("先给结论" / "Bottom line" / "Executive Summary" as H2), but only ~13/100 -- it is not the dominant pattern.
