dissection.performance:
bodhi 54.07 = rank 3. Per-dimension: Comprehensiveness 54.15, Insight 54.60, Instruction Following 54.41, Readability 51.87.

Its profile vs the pack:
- INSIGHT is its best dimension (54.60) -- 2nd highest on the whole board behind cellcog-max (57.08), ahead of WhaleCloud (55.33) and lunon (54.83). Against POLARIS's 42.38, that is a ~12-point gap on the 0.32-weight dimension.
- READABILITY is its WORST (51.87) and it does not care. The price of ~250 inline [[n]](url) hyperlinks per report. Note dalpha ranks 5th overall with the board's BEST readability (53.20) -- readability is not how you climb here.
- It achieves this with the SHORTEST reports in the top tier (~10.7k words vs cellcog's 23.9k). It is the highest insight-per-word system on the board.
- It is unusually FLAT: per-task insight ranges only 0.483-0.635. No blowups, no failures (100/100 scored). This consistency is itself an architectural signal -- the bespoke-rubric-per-report method transfers across all 22 fields.
- Within bodhi's own 100 tasks, the features that correlate (weakly, all |r|<0.3) with insight are: tables +0.21, uncited-inference-lines +0.18, unique sources +0.16. Length correlates with NOTHING (+0.05) and hurts readability (-0.21).
what_we_can_steal: THE BIG ONE -- ADOPT A TWO-REGISTER PROSE POLICY INSTEAD OF LIFTING SPAN-VERIFICATION. Do not weaken grounding: bodhi cites 72% of its substantive sentences, roughly what we do. Instead, ADD a second, syntactically-marked register. Keep span-verification mandatory for every sentence in the evidence register. Then permit a bolded, labelled INFERENCE line (`**Implication**:`, `**Mechanism**:`, `**Net verdict**:`) that is EXEMPT from span-verification and is REQUIRED to reference >=2 already-cited evidence units from the section. The bold label is what makes it safe and legible -- it tells reader and judge 'this is analysis, not a sourced fact'. This gives us cross-source inference without abandoning the fact-conveyor guarantee. Measured: 67% of bodhi's inference lines are uncited vs 32% of its evidence lines.

PER-REPORT BESPOKE ANALYTIC RUBRIC. After planning, have the model invent 2-4 NAMED analytic slots for THIS task (bodhi: 1720 distinct labels over 100 reports -- e.g. 'Restructuring implication', '统一解读', '传导机制', '为什么它强', '关键风险'). Then apply the SAME slots to every evidence unit in the report. Systematic repetition of a task-appropriate question is what makes the synthesis read as analysis rather than as decoration. This is cheap: it is one extra planning call producing a 4-item list.

GIVE THE COMPOSER THE WHOLE SOURCE LIBRARY, NOT SECTION-SCOPED SNIPPETS. Cross-source inference is IMPOSSIBLE if the writer only sees one section's sources. bodhi's citation-index-vs-position correlation is +0.279 (vs WhaleCloud's +0.872) -- it draws from the full pool starting in section 1. If we retrieve per-section, we have architecturally forbidden the very insight we are trying to buy.

OVER-RETRIEVE THEN CURATE. Build a numbered library of ~130-280 sources and let the writer cite only ~60 of them (bodhi leaves ~50% of numbered slots unused). Never-cited sources still shape the writer's judgement of what is consensus vs outlier. Do not prune the library to what will be cited.

SECTION ZERO = SCOPE CONTRACT, NOT EXECUTIVE SUMMARY. Open by declaring: our definition of the key term, the date cutoff, the evidence-inclusion rule ('only peer-reviewed journal articles'), and an evidence-strength taxonomy ('company self-report vs media vs regulatory filing'). This is likely a large part of bodhi's 54.41 instruction_following, and it is nearly free to add.

RESOLVE CONFLICT ARCHITECTURALLY, NOT WITH A TENSIONS SECTION. bodhi has the FEWEST conflict markers of the top systems (4.5/report) and almost never has a limitations section. It instead makes measurement-convention (口径) the FIRST section -- explaining WHY sources disagree -- so the whole report reads as a reconciliation. For any task where sources give different numbers, lead with 'here is why the numbers differ' rather than listing both and shrugging.

MAKE HEADINGS ARGUMENTATIVE. bodhi's 1186 H2s have a max repeat of 4 -- zero boilerplate outline. Headings assert claims ('not who's hot, but who survives the cycle'; 'who has real room and who is just superficially hot'). A claim-bearing heading is free insight surface that costs no tokens of body text. Kill any fixed section template we have.

STOP BUYING LENGTH. bodhi is rank 3 with the SHORTEST reports in the top tier (10.7k words, less than half of cellcog). Within its own 100 tasks, length correlates +0.05 with insight and -0.21 with readability. Our recent flywheel work chasing 8055 words is pushing on a rope. Spend the budget on inference lines, not on more sections.

GENRE-GATE THE TABLES. 66/100 bodhi reports have ZERO tables; tables appear ONLY for explicitly comparative tasks. Tabling everything (WhaleCloud, cellcog) is not what separates rank 3 from rank 7. But within bodhi, tables are its single strongest per-task correlate of insight (+0.21) -- so emit them when, and only when, the task is a comparison.

CLOSE IN SECOND PERSON, BOUND TO THE USER'S DECISION. bodhi's final sections are 'the most critical conclusion FOR YOUR question (condensed)', 'if you are choosing a career direction, who suits whom?', 'a company list you can use directly', 'the evidence gaps in THIS answer'. It answers a person, not a topic. Cheap to add; likely helps both insight and instruction_following.
unknowns: The model(s) behind Bodhi Deep Research. Publicis Sapient says only 'model-agnostic' and 'powered by Amazon Bedrock and AgentCore'. Bedrock implies Claude/Nova are available to it, but NOTHING confirms which model wrote these reports.

Whether the plan/outline is generated before or after retrieval. The stable global citation index proves retrieval precedes composition, but not whether the OUTLINE precedes retrieval. Both are consistent with the artifacts.

Whether there is a revision/critique pass. I found no textual evidence of one (no residue, no reference list, tight score band), but absence of evidence is not evidence of absence. UNVERIFIED either way.

Whether composition is literally one LLM call or a section-parallel writer with shared global source context. The citation-spread test rules out section-SCOPED RETRIEVAL, but not section-parallel WRITING against a shared library.
The number of search/tool calls, the retrieval depth, and any reranking. We only see ~130-280 numbered survivors, not the funnel above them.

Whether the bespoke bold labels are model-invented per report or drawn from a large internal library of analytic frames. 1720 distinct labels over 100 reports is consistent with either, though the tight fit to each task's domain favours per-report invention.

The Publicis Sapient product page's '#1 on DeepResearch Bench' claim directly contradicts their own '#3' social posts. Which (if either) refers to a real leaderboard state is unresolved -- do NOT quote either number.
Nothing about the 'Enterprise Context Graph' is verifiable from these reports; it is likely an enterprise-deployment feature not exercised by the benchmark.

systems.system: dalpha-deepresearch (Dalpha / 달파, Seoul-based AI agent startup; agent framework reportedly named "Cobra"). GPT-5.5 board: 53.10 overall, rank 5.
systems.reasoning:
I verified this against primary artifacts (the corpus + score files) and the two primary press sources, and tried to break it. It survives almost everything.

EPISTEMIC DISCIPLINE (the thing you asked me to police) — PASSES. The claim does NOT present an inferred architecture as documented design. It explicitly states "the architecture below is reverse-engineered from the 100 artifacts, not from any disclosure," tags RETRIEVAL "(inferred)" and PROCESS SHAPE "(inferred, medium confidence)," says "Treat 'world model' as marketing until a paper lands," and volunteers a negative result ("I found NO direct evidence of an adversarial critic pass"). The inference/documentation line is drawn correctly and held.

PUBLISHED RECORD — CONFIRMED against primaries. I fetched both URLs. etnews.com/20260507000224 confirms verbatim: Cobra Agent, MLE-Bench 79.11% vs CAIR MARS+ 62.67 / Baidu FM-Agent 64.44, "환경과 모델의 상호작용을 내부적으로 시뮬레이션하며 미래 결과를 예측하는 월드 모델 방법론" (world-model methodology), and that the paper is only "예정" (planned) — not published. thelec.net/11532 confirms the GIFT-Eval / DeepResearch Bench / DRB-II #1 claims with no scores and no architecture. Searches for a Dalpha/Cobra paper return only unrelated COBRA papers (Mamba VLM, contextual bandits, CHI26 social-agents). HAS PAPER: false is correct.

QUANTITATIVE CLAIMS — reproduced from the artifacts, mostly to the digit:
- Board: 53.10 overall, rank 5, Comp .5258 / Insight .5294 / IF .5387 / Read .5320 — exact; readability IS the board maximum.
- Headings: 3,339 total (exact match), uniqueness 0.895 (claimed 0.896); "Sources" last in 100/100; exec-summary opening 79/100 (claimed 78).
- 83/100 tables, 91/100 horizontal rules — both exact. Median 69 inline [n] markers — exact; density 15.7/1k (claimed 15.1).
- corr(words, insight) = 0.217 (claimed 0.21); corr(words, overall) = 0.262 (claimed 0.27) — exact.
- Median unique URLs: dalpha 30, gemini 106, bodhi 60, openai-DR 41, lunon 11 (claimed 12) — match. Shortest-report ordering holds.
- Conditional routers: I count 47/100 (claimed 48).

COMPOSITION MECHANISM — the load-bearing part, and it is confirmed in unusual detail, not hand-waved. id 44 contains literally every element claimed: a 口径 scope section; a parameter table whose third column is BASIS with citations [7][8]; a LOW/MID/HIGH scenario table yielding 约2.4万 / 3.4万–4.5万 / 4.8万–5.7万 (= the claimed 24k–57k, exact); an exec summary stating "约3万—6万根/年，中位判断约4万根/年左右" (= "30k-60k/yr, median judgment ~40k/yr", exact); the independent tender cross-check "石家庄…860根、合同金额144.48万元，折算约1,680元/根" with an explicit reconciliation of why it disagrees; and §3.1 titled "先说限制：公开份额不等于真实市场份额" with exactly four enumerated reasons, placed BEFORE the share tables, followed by tables labeled 样本A/样本B with sample total 约796.1万元 (= the claimed RMB 7.961M, exact) and a refusal to generalize. id 66 has the scope contract before any candidate, "not documented List view" cells, per-candidate Strengths/Weaknesses/Verdict, and an exec-summary 1-5 ranking IDENTICAL to the Bottom line ~4,000 words later. id 68 has Patterns A-F + router. id 1 has the 9-tier CN framework with 口径说明. Citation indices recur heavily across distant sections in id 44, supporting the "global bibliography, no per-section silos" argument.

DEFECTS FOUND (all peripheral, none touching the architecture):
1. FALSE sub-claim: id 72 "zero web URLs." The report contains 35 http(s) URLs — 34 are https://doi.org/... resolver links and 1 is academic.oup.com. The spirit (journal-only, DOI-based, no blogspam) is right; the literal statement is wrong.
2. NOT REPRODUCIBLE: "median paragraph 37 words." I get 27 (blank-line blocks) or 14 (per-line). Short-paragraph claim holds directionally; the number does not.
3. NOT REPRODUCIBLE: "40/100 evidence-gap statements" and "43/100 labeled assumptions." My best regexes reach 30 and ~25-30. The phenomenon is real and pervasive; the exact counts likely come from an unspecified classifier and should be treated as soft.
4. SOFT: micro-form comparison numbers are systematically inflated by ~20-40% relative to my tokenizer (dalpha median bullet 7 not 9; gemini 39 not 56; cellcog 19 not 23, 48% not 55% bold-led; median words 4,706 not 5,224). Every comparison's DIRECTION and ordering survives; the absolute figures are tokenizer-dependent.
5. INTERPRETIVE OVERREACH (not mislabeled fact, but unearned): "the single cheapest insight+instruction-following lever on the board" and "it wins on the RANKING itself" are causal claims with no ablation or per-feature regression behind them. They should read as hypotheses.

Bottom line: the architecture is inferred, is labeled as inferred, and every inference is anchored to artifact text I independently reproduced. No place crosses the line into stating reverse-engineered design as documented design. Fix the four numeric items above and the claim stands.

dissection.architecture:
PUBLISHED RECORD: essentially nothing. Searched arXiv, GitHub, HF, company site, and Korean-language press (달파, 딥리서치, 에이전트). There is NO paper, NO code, NO tech report, NO model/dataset card for dalpha-deepresearch. What exists is press: (a) TheElec (thelec.net/news/articleView.html?idxno=11532) — Dalpha claims #1 on GIFT-Eval, DeepResearch Bench and DRB-II with "a proprietary general-purpose agent framework" not task-specialized; no scores, no architecture, no third-party verification. (b) ETNews 2026-05-07 (etnews.com/20260507000224) — framework named "Cobra"; scored 79.11% on OpenAI MLE-Bench (vs Google CAIR MARS+ 62.67, Baidu FM-Agent 64.44); described as using a "world model methodology" that internally simulates environment-model interaction to predict future outcomes, enabling autonomous exploration and self-improvement; company says a paper is PLANNED but not published. Treat "world model" as marketing until a paper lands. So the architecture below is reverse-engineered from the 100 artifacts, not from any disclosure.

RETRIEVAL (inferred): deliberately SMALL and primary-source-biased. Median 30 unique URLs per report (range 7-92) vs gemini-2.5-pro 106, bodhi 60, openai-DR 41. Sources are official docs, GitHub repos, standards (GB/T), ministry statistics, journal DOIs — not blogspam. On the lit-review task (id 72) every one of 35 refs is a journal article with a DOI and zero web URLs; on the Obsidian task (id 66) sources are obsidian.md/help pages, plugin listings and repo manifest.json files. So retrieval is TARGETED AT AUTHORITATIVE ARTIFACTS and the source TYPE is selected by task genre (a genre-conditioned retrieval policy). Median 69 inline [n] markers over ~30 sources = each source is re-cited ~2.3x; citation density 15 markers/1k words, ~1 per 67 words.

OUTLINE: bespoke, not templated. Heading uniqueness across all 100 reports = 0.896 (2993 unique of 3339 headings). The only invariants are: H1 title (100%), an executive-summary-type opening section (78/100: "Executive summary"/"执行摘要"/"核心结论"/"结论摘要"/"Short answer"/"Bottom line"), a Conclusion (~65/100), and "## Sources" as the last section (100/100). Everything between is derived from the question. It writes ANSWER-SHAPED outlines, not TOPIC-SHAPED ones (e.g. id 68 K8s: "Why standard Cluster Autoscaler may not be enough" -> "Strategy taxonomy" -> "Patterns A-F" -> "Provider-specific" -> "Recommended architectures IF demand is calendar-predictable / IF event-driven / IF latency-critical").

LENGTH: the shortest report on the board by 2-4x. Median 5,224 words (EN 3,956 / CN 6,290) vs cellcog-max 20,957, gemini 16,144, lunon 14,998, WhaleCloud 10,296, bodhi 9,883. It beats gemini (49.98) by 3.1 pts with 1/3 the words and 1/4 the sources. corr(words, insight) within dalpha = 0.21 — length is nearly irrelevant to its own scores.

MICRO-FORM: median paragraph 37 words (short, one-claim paragraphs). Bullets are SHORT enumerations (median 9 words; only 20% bold-led) — unlike gemini (56-word bullets = bullet-as-paragraph) or cellcog (23 words, 55% bold-led). Argument lives in prose and tables; bullets only enumerate. 83/100 use tables, 91/100 use --- rules between sections. Reads as high-signal-per-word, which is why readability = 53.20, the HIGHEST of any system on the board.
dissection.composition_method:
THE KEY MECHANISM — "the inference lives in the SCHEMA, not in the sentences."

dalpha does NOT get insight by writing speculative sentences. Almost every declarative sentence is still tightly attached to a citation (15 markers/1k words). What it does instead is INVENT AN ORGANIZING FRAME that no source contains, and then populate that frame with cited facts. The novel/analytic content is carried by structure, not by unverifiable prose. Four concrete frame types, all observed:

1. SCOPE CONTRACT (口径 / "What X means"). ~31/100 open with a definitional section that states what counts and what doesn't, usually as an inclusion/exclusion table. id 44 opens with a table of five scope variants and marks each "included in main estimate / excluded, reported separately". id 66 opens with "What 'Notion-like multi-view database' means" and derives 4 required capabilities BEFORE naming any candidate. This is a judgment (it is an authorial decision) that reads as rigor, and it makes every later claim commensurable. It is the single cheapest insight+instruction-following lever on the board.

2. TRANSPARENT ARITHMETIC MODEL (the Fermi engine). This is how it produces numbers that exist in NO source without hallucinating. id 44 (annual demand for metro pantograph carbon strips, insight 0.570): (a) fleet denominator from official statistics [1][2][3]; (b) a PARAMETER TABLE where every row is (parameter | estimated value | BASIS — with citation or "this is an estimation convention, not official statistics"); (c) a LOW/MID/HIGH SCENARIO TABLE producing 24k-57k units; (d) plus new-build fitment, giving "30k-60k/yr, median judgment ~40k/yr"; (e) an INDEPENDENT CROSS-CHECK against a real procurement tender (860 units / RMB 1.4448M -> RMB 1,680/unit) and an explicit reconciliation of why the check disagrees; (f) a monetary estimate = quantity x unit price. Every INPUT is span-verifiable. The COMBINATION step is explicitly labeled as authorial estimate. That is cross-source inference done in a form that survives a fact-checker.

3. EVIDENCE-STATUS LEDGER (turning non-findings into insight). 40/100 contain an explicit "the evidence cannot establish X" block, and crucially it is placed BEFORE the weak numbers, not buried. id 44 §3.1: "public tender share != real market share" with four enumerated reasons, then presents share tables labeled "Sample A" / "Sample B" with the sample total (RMB 7.961M) and refuses to generalize. id 66's comparison matrix contains cells reading "No documented distinct List view", "Not verified", "no separate Calendar verified" — and it then DERIVES a conclusion from the absence ("therefore not a full four-view replacement"). Absence of evidence is converted into an analytic distinction instead of an omission. This is why an extraction-grounded system does not have to lie to gain insight: "not documented" is itself a verifiable, load-bearing claim.

4. VERDICT + ROUTER LAYER (commitment). It commits to an answer. Per-item "### Verdict" blocks (id 66: every candidate gets Strengths / Weaknesses / Verdict), a global "## Bottom line" / "最终判断" / "结论先说" / "Short answer", and a CONDITIONAL ROUTER: 48/100 contain "Best for X -> use Y" / "If demand is calendar-predictable -> ..." / "如果你...". This is a decision function over the evidence, which no single source supplies. Note it wins on the RANKING itself: the id 66 exec summary is a ranked 1-5 shortlist with one-line reasons, produced before any evidence is shown.

PROCESS SHAPE (inferred, medium confidence): the invariant skeleton (title -> exec summary -> bespoke body -> conclusion -> Sources) plus near-total heading uniqueness plus the fact that the exec summary is a ranked/quantified ANSWER that presupposes the whole body implies a plan-then-write pipeline where the OUTLINE IS SYNTHESIZED FIRST FROM AN EVIDENCE LEDGER, then sections are written against it, then the exec summary is written LAST (or rewritten last) from the finished verdicts. Evidence for a late/global pass: exec summary numbers, the mid-report scenario table and the conclusion agree exactly in id 44; the id 66 exec summary ranking is identical to the Bottom-line ranking 4,000 words later. Evidence AGAINST a heavy multi-agent parallel-section writer: no section-boundary repetition, no redundant re-introductions, uniform voice, no per-section source silos (the same [n] indices recur across distant sections, so the bibliography is GLOBAL and fixed before writing, not per-section). I found NO direct evidence of an adversarial critic pass; the "not verified / not documented" language is better explained by a verification pass over CLAIMS (each cell/parameter checked against a source, with the check's outcome written into the text) than by a prose critic.

WHAT IT DOES NOT DO: it does not pad. It does not template. It does not bury argument in bullets. It does not maximize sources. It is last among the top-5 on comprehensiveness (52.58) and first on the whole board on readability (53.20) — it deliberately trades breadth for tightness and still ranks 5th.

dissection.performance:
GPT-5.5 board: overall 53.10 (rank 5). Dimension profile is the OPPOSITE of the rest of the top-5: comprehensiveness 52.58 (LOWEST of the top 5 — cellcog 56.34, WhaleCloud 55.14, bodhi 54.15, lunon 53.42), insight 52.94 (also lowest of top-5), instruction-following 53.87 (2nd on the whole board), readability 53.20 (HIGHEST ON THE ENTIRE BOARD — nobody else clears 52.5; cellcog 51.94, gemini 49.58).

So dalpha is not a "more of everything" system. It is the efficiency outlier: it is the shortest report on the board (5.2k words vs a 10-21k pack) with the second-fewest sources, and it converts that into the best readability and near-top instruction-following while sacrificing raw coverage. It out-scores the gemini reference by +3.12 overall and +3.02 insight using ~1/3 the words and ~1/4 the URLs.

Relative to POLARIS (43.82, insight 42.38): the gap to dalpha's insight is +10.6 points and it is NOT explained by length, source count, or coverage — dalpha has less of all three than several systems we already beat on those axes. The gap is explained entirely by the four frame mechanisms above (scope contract, arithmetic model, evidence-status ledger, verdict/router). This is the single best system on the board to copy, because it is the only top-5 system whose advantage does not require us to write 20k words.
what_we_can_steal: THE BIG ONE — move inference into the SCHEMA, not the sentences. dalpha keeps ~every declarative sentence citation-attached (15 markers/1k words) yet scores insight 52.94. The non-extractable content is carried by the FRAME: the axis of comparison, the taxonomy, the arithmetic, the verdict. We can keep span verification 100% intact and still gain insight, because the frame is authorial by construction and no verifier should be asked to span-check it. Our 'extraction-grounded' constraint is NOT the cause of our insight score; our lack of an authorial layer is.

SCOPE CONTRACT (口径 section). Open with a section that defines what counts and what does not, ideally as an inclusion/exclusion table (dalpha: 31/100, and it is the FIRST body section on the hardest tasks). It costs ~200 words, is fully verifiable, and it is simultaneously an insight signal (you made a defensible analytic choice) and an instruction-following signal (you addressed prompt ambiguity explicitly). Cheapest lever we have.

TRANSPARENT ARITHMETIC MODEL for any quantity the sources do not state. Pattern from id 44, copy it literally: (1) denominator from an official stat [cite]; (2) a PARAMETER TABLE with columns (parameter | value | BASIS) where every row is either cited or explicitly stamped 'estimation convention, not official statistics'; (3) a LOW/MID/HIGH scenario table; (4) a stated range plus a single committed median judgment; (5) an INDEPENDENT cross-check against a different source type and an explicit reconciliation of the discrepancy. Every input is span-verifiable; only the multiplication is authorial, and it is labeled. This is cross-source inference that a fact-checker cannot fault.

EVIDENCE-STATUS LEDGER — make our verification failures into content. Today a span we cannot verify becomes an omission (pure loss). dalpha writes 'No public official statistic can answer this directly', 'no distinct List view is documented', 'Not verified', 'public tender share != real market share, for four reasons', and then DERIVES conclusions from the absence. Our verifier already knows exactly which claims failed verification — we are throwing away the highest-insight-density signal we produce. Emit it: a comparison matrix whose cells carry evidence status, and a 'what the evidence cannot establish' block placed BEFORE the weak numbers.

COMMIT TO A VERDICT. Per-item '### Verdict' blocks and a global '## Bottom line' / '最终判断' / 'Short answer'. dalpha ranks its options in the executive summary BEFORE showing evidence, then re-states the identical ranking at the end. A ranking is a claim no source makes — and it is the highest-yield insight token per word on this benchmark.

CONDITIONAL ROUTER (48/100). Close with 'Best for X -> use Y' / 'If demand is calendar-predictable -> pattern A; if latency-critical -> pattern E'. It is a decision function over the evidence, cheap to generate from the comparison matrix, and it reads as expert judgment.

INVENT NAMED ABSTRACTIONS. id 68 coins 'Pattern A-F' and a 'strategy taxonomy' that appear in no source, then fills them with cited facts. Naming the axes IS the synthesis. Our pipeline currently only ever reuses the sources' own framing.

STOP PADDING. dalpha is the SHORTEST report on the board (5.2k median words, EN 3.9k) and ranks 5th, beating the 16k-word gemini reference on every dimension. If POLARIS is generating length to chase comprehensiveness, that is negative-value: comprehensiveness is dalpha's WORST dimension and it still ranks 5. Reasoning-per-word is what the RACE judge rewards.

SHORT PARAGRAPHS, SHORT BULLETS. Median paragraph 37 words, median bullet 9 words, bullets used only for enumeration and never as paragraph containers. This is the mechanical driver of dalpha's board-leading readability 53.20. gemini's 56-word bullets correlate with its 49.58 readability.

GENRE-CONDITIONED RETRIEVAL + CITATION FORMAT. On the 'cite only high-quality English journal articles' task (id 72) dalpha cites 35 DOIs and ZERO web URLs; on the software task it cites official docs, plugin listings and repo manifest.json. Source TYPE is selected from the prompt. Small, authoritative source set (median 30 URLs), each re-cited ~2.3x — not a 100-URL dump. Retrieval breadth is not the win condition.

GLOBAL BIBLIOGRAPHY, THEN WRITE. The same [n] indices recur across distant sections, so the reference list is fixed before composition (not per-section silos). Freeze the evidence ledger, synthesize the outline FROM the ledger, write sections against it, then write the executive summary LAST from the finished verdicts so the headline numbers/rankings match the body exactly (they do, in every report I checked).
unknowns: Whether there is any paper at all. There is NONE as of now: no arXiv, no GitHub, no HF card, no tech report for dalpha-deepresearch or 'Cobra'. ETNews says a paper is planned. Any architecture claim about dalpha beyond what I derived from the 100 artifacts is speculation.
The 'world model' claim is press-only (ETNews, company-sourced) and refers to their MLE-Bench result, NOT to the deep-research submission. Do not assume it is in the report pipeline. Unverified.

Whether Dalpha actually holds #1 on DeepResearch Bench / DRB-II as TheElec claims — on OUR GPT-5.5 board it is rank 5 (53.10). The #1 claim is a company announcement with no scores and no third-party verification. Do not repeat it.
Number of agent turns, tool-call budget, search engine, and base model are all unknown. Median 30 unique URLs is a lower bound on retrieval (pages fetched but not cited are invisible to me).

Whether a critic/revision pass exists. I found no direct textual evidence of one. Internal consistency between the exec summary and the body is strong, which is consistent EITHER with a late global exec-summary write OR with a revision loop. Cannot distinguish from artifacts alone.
Whether the exec-summary-last ordering is real or an artifact of a plan-first pipeline. Inferred, medium confidence.

Whether the 'not verified / not documented' language comes from an explicit claim-verification stage or is just a prompted style. The former is my read (it is applied per-cell in matrices, which is stage-like), but it is not proven.

systems.system: sourcery (rank 6, 51.17) = "Sourcery", an independent Node.js deep-research CLI by Nitin Bansal (GitHub @freakynit). Identity CONFIRMED: the public Sourcery reports at sourcery-deep-research.pagey.site use the exact same 11-section scaffold as our 100 benchmark artifacts. It is NOT the Python code-review tool sourcery.ai (name collision).
systems.reasoning:
IDENTITY + PUBLISHED PIPELINE: CONFIRMED from primary source, fetched live 2026-07-13. https://sourcery-deep-research.pagey.site/ states the 12 stages verbatim (Understand, Plan, Generate, Search, Clean, Extract, Expand, Cross-check "Find Contradictions", Remember, Synthesize "Build Draft Insights", Verify "Protect Citation Integrity", Deliver), "Sourcery is a Node.js CLI", "Under $0.10 per report — including residential proxy costs for anti-bot bypass", built by Nitin Bansal, repo github.com/freakynit/sourcery. about.html states verbatim: "SQLite persistence — all state (runs, sources, claims, contradictions, memory) stored locally"; "Citations aren't decorative - they're deterministically rebuilt from the actual source list"; "Sourcery doesn't smooth over conflicts; contradictions between sources are surfaced explicitly"; "LLM-agnostic — works with any OpenAI-compatible inference endpoint". curl confirms github.com/freakynit/sourcery = HTTP 404. The public report the-coming-ai-cybersecurity-crisis.pagey.site returns the identical H2 scaffold (Executive Summary > Key Questions Answered > Core Findings > Contradictions & Debates > Deep Analysis > Implications > Future Outlook [Optimistic/Base Case/Pessimistic + probability assessment] > Unknowns & Open Questions > Evidence Map, 19 refs, [N] -> #reference-N), which does pin the leaderboard entry to this system. sourceryintel.com is marketing-only as claimed.

ARTIFACT STATS I REPRODUCED (scripts over drb_corpus/gpt55_board/sourcery.jsonl, n=100): 86/100 identical H2 order (14 distinct orders total); 11.0 H2 mean; refs mean 11.91, median 12, max 19; tables 100/100; Future Outlook triad 99/99; 50/50 Chinese reports keep English H2 (48/50 strictly; 2 have some CJK H2); Core Findings anchored 14,904 vs 335 bare; "Source N" leak in 65 reports / 1,601 occurrences (claim said 64/1,470 — close); Contradictions & Debates mean 6.63 named tensions; Core Findings mean 4.74 distinct sources per subsection (claim: 4.94), 70% cite >=3 (claim 74%), 12% single-source (claim 15%); section word ordering Core Findings (~42% of body) >> Deep Analysis > Contradictions > Key Questions > rest. Heading inconsistency "Contradictions & Debates" (96) vs "Contradictions and Debates" (3) confirmed, i.e. no global normalization pass.

WHAT IS WRONG OR OVERSTATED:
1) FALSE as stated: "'Unknowns & Open Questions' uses bare [N] while EVERY OTHER SECTION uses HTML anchors." Unknowns is 93.9% bare (876 bare / 57 anchored, in 90/100 reports), but Implications is 42.2% bare (736 bare / 1,007 anchored, in 67/100 reports) and Executive Summary is 12% bare (243/1,786, 34 reports). The linkification anomaly is graded across sections, not a single-section miss. The multi-pass inference survives (arguably strengthened), but the stated fact does not.
2) FALSE as stated: Evidence Map "always a table: Theme x Strongest Evidence / Moderate Evidence / Weak-or-Absent Evidence". It is always a table (99/99), but there are 94 DISTINCT header rows across those 99 tables; only 7/99 use a Strong/Moderate/Weak triad; 72 have any strength/confidence column, 34 a gaps column. The Evidence Map schema is essentially free-form per report — which cuts against the "fixed schema" framing at table level.
3) Wrong counts: Contradictions, Unknowns, Deep Analysis, Implications and Future Outlook each appear in 99/100 reports, not 96/96/95. (The claim's own text is internally inconsistent here: section 1 says 100/100 core set, 4c says 99/100, the artifact bullet says 96/95.)
4) Example in 4(b) does not support 4(b): the task-72 sentence "The ratio between the most conservative and most optimistic estimates exceeds 10:1, driven primarily by divergent assumptions about task coverage, diffusion speed, and complementary investment rather than conflicting empirical data" cites [8] and only [8] (twice). It is a cross-source-computed claim, but it is NOT "cited to both sides".
5) Unverified magnitudes: sentence-level coverage 65.7% (n=17,160) and 40% multi-source cited sentences did not reproduce under my segmentation (54.9% coverage over n=31,008; 29.7% of cited sentences have >=2 sources). Direction (coverage well under 100%; a large minority of cited sentences are multi-source) holds; the exact figures are method-dependent and should not be quoted as measured constants without publishing the tokenizer/splitter. Same for the 11,910-word mean (I get 10,338 with a CJK-char + Latin-word counter) and the per-section word budgets — ordering reproduces, absolute values are tokenizer-dependent.

WHERE THE INFERENCE/DOCUMENTATION LINE IS CROSSED (the main methodological problem):
- "LOAD-BEARING FACT: ... sources / claims / contradictions are separate first-class SQLite tables. ... Synthesis then operates on claim OBJECTS and their RELATIONS, not on raw retrieved text." Only the first half is documented. The site says state (runs, sources, claims, contradictions, memory) is stored in SQLite — it does NOT say separate first-class tables, and it does NOT say synthesis reads claim objects. The vendor's own words for stage 10 are "Build Draft Insights" / "builds draft insights across EVIDENCE CHUNKS" — "evidence chunks" is if anything evidence AGAINST a pure claim-object synthesis and for chunk-level text. This is presented as documented design and is not; the repo is 404/private so it cannot be checked at all. Cross-check (8) running BEFORE Synthesize (10) IS documented and is confirmed.
- Section 4's entire composition model (schema-driven, section-scoped composition against a claim store; "sections are generated and stitched separately, then a deterministic citation-rebuild pass runs over them"; "proof the composer sees a numbered SOURCE CARD POOL") is inference from output text stated as fact. The vendor publishes NOTHING about report structure, section templates, or per-section generation. The one piece with vendor backing is the deterministic citation rebuild ("deterministically rebuilt from the actual source list") — that one is documented. The "Source N" prose leak is strong artifact evidence for a numbered source pool, but "proof" is too strong: it is consistent with numbered sources being injected into a single-pass prompt too.
- Section 5 ("no global revision/critique loop") is a reasonable negative inference from surviving heading inconsistencies and the linkification gap, but it is an inference; the Verify stage exists and is documented, and absence of a prose-critique loop is not documented anywhere.

NET: the vendor pipeline, cost, stack, SQLite state contents, deterministic citation rebuild, contradiction surfacing and stage ORDER are CONFIRMED from primary source. The corpus-level structural statistics are CONFIRMED (with three specific numbers wrong). The "claim database, not document buffer / synthesis over claim objects and relations" load-bearing framing and the whole section-scoped composition model are INFERENCE presented as documented design, and two supporting statistics (single-section bare-cite anomaly; fixed Evidence Map schema) are false as written.

Both are segmentation-dependent; report as "roughly one third to one half of sentences are uncited connective/interpretive prose" rather than as exact constants. NOTE: the showcased task-72 cross-source sentence ("...ratio ... exceeds 10:1 ... driven primarily by divergent assumptions about task coverage, diffusion speed, and complementary investment rather than conflicting empirical data") is cited to [8] ONLY, so it does not demonstrate a relation cited to both sides. - INFERRED (negative): no global revision pass over the finished draft — "Contradictions & Debates" (96) vs "Contradictions and Debates" (3) heading variants and the uneven linkification survive to output. The documented "critique" is upstream (Cross-check over claims); absence of a downstream prose-critique loop is inferred, not documented.

dissection.system: sourcery (rank 6, 51.17) = "Sourcery", an independent Node.js deep-research CLI by Nitin Bansal (GitHub @freakynit). Identity CONFIRMED: the public Sourcery reports at sourcery-deep-research.pagey.site use the exact same 11-section scaffold as our 100 benchmark artifacts. It is NOT the Python code-review tool sourcery.ai (name collision).
dissection.architecture: No paper / arXiv / tech report. But the author publishes a 12-step pipeline on the product site (primary source). Repo github.com/freakynit/sourcery is announced but still 404/private as of 2026-07-13. PUBLISHED PIPELINE (vendor stage names): 1 Understand (deconstruct question: hidden assumptions, ambiguities, key dimensions) -> 2 Plan (sub-questions, evidence requirements, LIKELY CONTROVERSIES, source types) -> 3 Generate (multi-angle queries, deliberately incl. skeptical/critical angles) -> 4 Search (crawl, residential proxies) -> 5 Clean (local HTML/PDF extraction) -> 6 Extract ("facts, numbers, dates, and uncertainty signals into structured notes") -> 7 Expand (recursively follow cited papers/links/datasets/unanswered questions) -> 8 Cross-check ("surfaces contradictions and conflicting interpretations") -> 9 Remember (SQLite persists "runs, sources, claims, contradictions, memory"; cross-run source memory; resumable) -> 10 Synthesize ("builds draft insights across evidence chunks") -> 11 Verify ("citations map deterministically to real sources") -> 12 Deliver (Markdown). LLM-agnostic (any OpenAI-compatible endpoint), <$0.10/report. LOAD-BEARING FACT: state is a CLAIM DATABASE, not a document buffer. sources / claims / contradictions are separate first-class SQLite tables. Contradiction detection is its OWN pipeline stage that runs over the claim store BEFORE any prose is written. Synthesis then operates on claim OBJECTS and their RELATIONS, not on raw retrieved text. RETRIEVAL PROFILE (measured, 100 artifacts): only 11.9 refs/report (median 12, max 19) but 11,910 words/report -> ~31 citation instances per distinct source. It mines FEW sources VERY deeply (the opposite of breadth-maximizing systems). Task 72 report says outright: "synthesizes 18 English-language academic sources". CITATION MECHANICS: body cites are HTML anchors [N] pointing at in the References list. Zero raw URLs in body. This is the "deterministically rebuilt" citation layer -- a programmatic post-pass, not LLM-emitted.
dissection.composition_method:
SCHEMA-DRIVEN, SECTION-SCOPED COMPOSITION AGAINST A CLAIM STORE. Not a free outline.

1) FIXED GLOBAL TEMPLATE, genre-invariant. 86/100 reports have the IDENTICAL H2 sequence; 100/100 share the core set: TOC > Executive Summary > Key Questions Answered > Core Findings > Contradictions & Debates > Deep Analysis > Implications > Future Outlook > Unknowns & Open Questions > Evidence Map > References. Future Outlook is ALWAYS Optimistic / Base Case / Pessimistic (98/100). It does NOT adapt structure to task genre -- Chinese-language reports (50/100) keep ENGLISH H2 headings while writing Chinese bodies. Same template on academic lit reviews, market sizing, and M&A analysis.

2) SECTION WORD BUDGET (measured): Core Findings 4,777w (40% of report, 7.8 subsections, 81% contain tables) >> Deep Analysis 1,627w > Contradictions 981w > Key Questions 808w > Evidence Map 632w (100% a table) > Executive Summary 595w (pure prose, no bullets/tables) > Future Outlook 586w > Implications 656w > Unknowns 521w.

3) EVIDENCE OF MULTI-PASS / SECTION-SCOPED GENERATION, not one global pass: citation FORMAT differs by section. Corpus-wide, "Unknowns & Open Questions" uses bare [4][7][11] (907 bare vs 57 anchored) while every other section uses HTML anchors (Core Findings 14,904 anchored vs 340 bare). The linkification/verify pass systematically misses one section -> sections are generated and stitched separately, then a deterministic citation-rebuild pass runs over them. Also 64/100 reports LEAK the internal source labels into prose ("Source 4 documents a 17% decline...", 1,470 occurrences) -- proof the composer sees a numbered SOURCE CARD POOL, not raw text.

4) HOW SYNTHESIS IS PRODUCED -- the actual answer:
 (a) The outline is THEMATIC, not per-source. Core Findings subsections draw a mean of 4.94 distinct sources each; 74% cite >=3 sources; only 15% are single-source. So each writing unit is a THEME with a bundle of claims from many sources.
 (b) Insight is emitted as an explicit RELATION between claims, and the relation itself is cited to both sides. 40% of all cited sentences cite >=2 distinct sources. Example (task 72): "The ratio between the most conservative and most optimistic estimates exceeds 10:1, driven by divergent ASSUMPTIONS about task coverage and diffusion speed rather than conflicting empirical data." That is a computed cross-source claim, still fully anchored.
 (c) THREE DEDICATED SYNTHESIS SECTIONS that are renderings of pipeline stages, not creative writing: "Contradictions & Debates" (99/100; 6.6 named tensions each; mean 3.78 distinct sources per tension; each tension states thesis vs antithesis with cites, then RESOLVES it -- e.g. "These findings are not necessarily contradictory -- they reflect different levels of analysis... AI can be a net complement in aggregate while being a severe substitute for particular occupations"); "Evidence Map" (99/100, always a table: Theme x Strongest Evidence / Moderate Evidence / Weak-or-Absent Evidence, with cites in each cell -- a rendering of evidence STRENGTH and GAPS); "Unknowns & Open Questions" (99/100, ~10 numbered gaps, each grounded in what sources FAIL to cover: "Source 4 explicitly lacks data on actual wages").
 (d) Uncited connective prose IS allowed: sentence-level citation coverage is only 65.7% (n=17,160 sentences). ~1/3 of sentences are interpretation, framing, and transition with no span behind them. It does not span-verify every sentence.
 (e) Hedging/contrast markers: 24.8 per report; 68 percent-figures per report. Dense, quantitative, and explicitly uncertain.

5) NO evidence of a global revision/critique loop over the finished draft (heading inconsistencies like "Contradictions & Debates" vs "Contradictions and Debates" and the missed linkification in one section survive to output). The "critique" is upstream, in the Cross-check stage over claims -- not downstream over prose.

dissection.performance:
On our GPT-5.5 board: overall 51.17 | comprehensiveness 50.53 | insight 52.22 | instruction_following 51.06 | readability 49.68.

Shape: INSIGHT IS ITS BEST DIMENSION and its only clearly above-reference one (+2.24 over the 49.98 gemini reference); readability is its WORST and is BELOW the reference (49.68). This is a system that buys insight at the cost of readability -- the fixed 11-section scaffold, the 4-column Evidence Map table, English headings on Chinese reports, and 372 inline anchor-cites per report all hurt readability. It is also the SHORTEST of the top 6 (11.9k words vs cellcog-max 22.3k, lunon 15.6k) and the most source-frugal (11.9 refs), yet beats gemini/openai on insight. Insight-per-source and insight-per-word are its differentiator, not scale. Its comprehensiveness (50.53) is the weakest of the top 6 -- consistent with mining 12 sources very deeply rather than covering 50.
what_we_can_steal: THE ONE THING: move inference from the SENTENCE level to the CLAIM level. Extract claims into a store (claim, value, source_id, uncertainty signal), then run a separate CROSS-CHECK stage that computes RELATIONS between claims (agrees / contradicts / different-level-of-analysis / different-assumption). A synthesis sentence is then a rendering of a relation between two span-verified claims and cites BOTH -- so it is still fully grounded, but it is NOT extractable from any single span. This is exactly the move that unblocks our insight score without abandoning grounding. 40% of sourcery's cited sentences cite >=2 sources; ours are ~all single-source.

ADD THREE DEDICATED SYNTHESIS SECTIONS, each a direct rendering of a pipeline stage, not free prose: (1) 'Contradictions & Debates' -- ~7 named tensions, each stating thesis-with-cites vs antithesis-with-cites and then an explicit RESOLUTION sentence ('these are not contradictory -- they operate at different levels of analysis'); (2) 'Evidence Map' -- a table of Theme x Strongest / Moderate / Weak-or-Absent evidence, cites in every cell (this scores insight AND comprehensiveness for ~630 words); (3) 'Unknowns & Open Questions' -- ~10 numbered gaps, each grounded NEGATIVELY in what a specific source fails to cover ('Source 4 explicitly lacks data on wages'). Negative grounding is span-verifiable and reads as insight.

PLAN FOR CONTROVERSY UP FRONT. Sourcery's PLAN stage explicitly maps 'likely controversies' and its QUERY stage deliberately generates skeptical/critical-angle queries. If you never retrieve the counter-evidence, no downstream synthesis stage can find a tension. Our retrieval is probably consensus-biased.

RELAX 100% SPAN COVERAGE. Sourcery cites only 65.7% of sentences; the other third is interpretation and transition. Our all-or-nothing span rule is likely what forbids the connective tissue that reads as thought. Propose a two-tier rule: FACTUAL sentences must be span-verified; RELATIONAL sentences must cite every claim they relate (both sides); CONNECTIVE sentences may be uncited but may not introduce new facts.

DEPTH OVER BREADTH: 12 sources, ~31 citation instances each. Mining one source 30 times forces you to use its assumptions, methods, sample sizes, and limitations -- which is where the tension material lives. Consider capping sources and raising per-source extraction depth (methods, N, limitations, stated uncertainty) rather than adding sources.

MAKE CITATION A DETERMINISTIC POST-PASS. Body cites are HTML anchors rebuilt from the source list, so the writer LLM never has to hallucinate a reference. Decouple 'write with source ids' from 'render citations'.

A GENRE-INVARIANT FIXED TEMPLATE IS ENOUGH TO RANK 6. 86/100 identical section order, same scaffold for lit reviews and M&A. We do not need per-task outline planning to beat our current 43.82 -- but note the cost: sourcery's readability (49.68) is below the reference, so DO adapt heading language to the prompt language (it does not, and pays for it).
Section word budget as an explicit contract: Core Findings ~40% of the report, everything else 5-14%. Prevents the exec-summary-bloat / thin-body failure.
unknowns: The code is NOT public: github.com/freakynit/sourcery returns 404 as of 2026-07-13, despite the site saying 'source code publishing soon'. Everything about the internals below the 12 stage names is inferred from artifacts, not read.

WHICH LLM(s) it uses. Site says only 'LLM-agnostic / any OpenAI-compatible endpoint'. The benchmark submission's actual model is unknown. Given <$0.10/report it cannot be a frontier model at high token counts -- but that cost claim is vendor-stated and unverified.

Whether the fixed 11-section template is a hard-coded prompt scaffold or an LLM-chosen outline that happens to converge. 86/100 identical order strongly implies hard-coded, but we have not seen the prompt.
Whether 'Cross-check' contradiction detection is an LLM call over claim pairs, an embedding/clustering step, or rule-based on extracted numeric ranges. Unknown.
Whether each section is a separate LLM call. The per-section citation-format divergence and 'Source N' leakage make section-scoped generation very likely, but we cannot prove it from text alone.
Whether any revision/critic pass exists at all. We see no evidence of one over the prose; 'Verify' is described only as citation-to-source mapping, not factual critique.
Its FACT/citation-accuracy numbers are absent from our board ('-' columns), so we cannot confirm its grounding claims ('0 hallucinated claims') empirically.

systems.reasoning:
VERIFIED BY RE-EXECUTION (I recomputed everything from the JSONL, not trusting the numbers).

== WHAT IS SOLID ==
1. All four primary-source quotes are VERBATIM. I extracted the system card PDF locally (pypdf, 36pp) after WebFetch's parse failed — same failure the author admitted. s1 "powered by an early version of OpenAI o3 that is optimized for web browsing" and the whole s2 RL/rubric/"chain-of-thought model as a grader" passage are exact. gemini.google self-critique sentence: exact. ai.google.dev "Plan -> Search -> Read -> Iterate -> Output" + "autonomously plans": exact. blog.google plan/iterate/1M: exact. The headline "two genuinely different architectures" is well-supported.
2. Scores exact. Structure stats reproduce almost perfectly: words 6727/5705 vs 4743/3192 (exact), H3 11.4 vs 2.8 (exact), tables 2.5/0.5 (exact), URLs 113 vs 43 (exact), bullets 187 vs 28 (exact once numbered lists counted), exec-summary 0/100 for OpenAI (exact).
3. Artifacts real: task 51 derived outline (incl. "6.1 Forecasting Approach"), task 53 ADIA/GPFG conflict, task 63 quote all present.
4. The length negative result reproduces EXACTLY: 38 OpenAI reports <1500w, 0.4714 vs 0.4826, gap 1.12pts. "Chasing 8000 words won't move insight" stands.
5. The killer fact stands: Gemini 38.8% uncited sentences vs OpenAI 79.7%, yet Gemini scores higher on insight. Uncited prose is NOT insight. Robust.

== WHAT BREAKS ==
A. THE MONOTONICITY INFERENCE IS INVERTED — the most serious defect. The claim reads monotonicity 0.63 as "only weakly linear => the numbered source list is a PRE-EXISTING enumeration of the retrieved corpus, and the writer cites INTO it from any point." I ran that exact hypothesis as a permutation null. A pre-existing enumeration (numbering independent of text order) predicts Spearman 0.02 / frac-increasing 0.51. OBSERVED: Spearman 0.59 (median 0.62), with 59/72 reports above 0.4. The author's own frac-increasing metric (I reproduce it: 0.60) is an insensitive adjacent-pairs statistic; the rank correlation shows citation numbers ARE substantially assigned in order of first use, which REFUTES the pre-existing-enumeration story. The conclusion (single global-context write) probably survives — sequential single-pass writing with a bibliography numbered in first-use order predicts exactly what I see — but it survives DESPITE the stated evidence, not because of it. The real support is cross-section reuse (I confirm 29% of sources cross 2+ H2 sections, 4.14 cite-tokens/source), which the claim treats as secondary.

B. THE HEADLINE PARAGRAPH MECHANISM HALF-FAILS. "Claim-evidence-warrant" is called "the finding that matters most for POLARIS." The uncited analytic OPENING is real and strong (pos0 66.8% uncited in my run — even more pronounced than claimed). But the uncited synthesis CODA does not reproduce: in my measurement the LAST sentence is the MOST-cited position (39.1% uncited, the minimum), not an uncited inference. The claim's profile is a U-shape (41.0/35.0/34.3/34.5/38.8); mine is open-uncited-then-increasingly-cited. The "25.8% cited-body+uncited-coda" figure comes out 12.3%, and its validation stat — coda paragraphs 28% vs 18% likely to carry tension language — comes out 21% vs 20%, i.e. NO effect. The task-63 example is a real n=1 anecdote, not a demonstrated pattern.

C. ARCHITECTURE FROM SILENCE, STATED AS FACT. "NO planner module, NO separate writer, NO critic, NO outline artifact" is inferred from a SAFETY system card that never discusses inference-time architecture. I grepped the full card: 0 hits for planner/subagent/multi-agent/self-critique (the 10 "critic" hits are all the word "Critical" in risk tables). Absence in a safety document is weak evidence of architectural absence — the card never denies a pipeline. This is a reasonable inference but it is presented as documented design; that line IS crossed. Relatedly, the "brief clarifying-question turn" is nowhere in the card (0 hits for clarify/clarifying) — that's product behavior, unsourced.

D. "Works cited" is a fabricated label. The literal string appears in 0/100 Gemini reports; there is no References/Bibliography/Sources heading in any of them. The numbered bibliography is real (98/100) but is an UNHEADED trailing numbered list.

E. GEMINI'S 0.50s ARE BY CONSTRUCTION, AND IT IS 7th OF 10. Gemini scores 0.5001/0.4992/0.5022/0.4958/0.4998 — dead 0.50 on every dimension because it IS the RACE reference scoring against itself. The author flags it's the reference but still uses its scores as evidence its architecture is superior. Six systems on this very board BEAT it (cellcog-max 55.78, WhaleCloud 54.78, bodhi 54.07, lunon 53.51, dalpha 53.10, sourcery 51.17). Holding up the 7th-place system as the architecture to copy is a material omission for the practical conclusion.

F. CURRENCY. Sources are Dec 2024 / Feb 2025; the Gemini artifacts are date-stamped "访问时间为 四月 28, 2025" (accessed 28 Apr 2025) — they are DeepResearch Bench's released outputs, not freshly generated. Nothing here is 2026. Fine as a description of the systems AS BENCHMARKED, but the claim writes in the present tense and never dates the artifacts.

G. Minor numeric drift: H3-per-H2 "gemini 1.48" reproduces under no convention I tried (aggregate 2.19, per-report 2.24; OpenAI 0.52 is right) — direction is understated, not overstated. "Executive summary 14/100" → 8 as a heading, 12 as any mention. The task-63 quote, presented as "verbatim," was silently reformatted (bare digits 22/1 → "[22]"/"[1]") and one clause dropped — ironic given the claim itself documents that Gemini uses bare digits.

== NET ==
The architectural dichotomy, the primary sourcing, the structural forensics, the grounding-vs-insight inversion, and the length negative result all hold up. But two of the three inferences the author bills as most decision-relevant — the "pre-existing enumeration / global-context write" citation forensic and the "uncited synthesis coda" paragraph mechanism — are contradicted or unreproduced by my re-analysis, and the OpenAI "no pipeline" architecture is inference-from-silence dressed as documented design. PARTIAL, not CONFIRMED.
systems.corrected:
CORRECTED CLAIM (changes marked):

TWO GENUINELY DIFFERENT ARCHITECTURES — CONFIRMED, with the caveat that OpenAI's side is INFERRED, not documented.

OPENAI DR: The system card (verbatim, verified) says the model is "an early version of OpenAI o3 that is optimized for web browsing" and learned to "write comprehensive reports through reinforcement learning training on these browsing tasks," graded "against the ground truth answers or rubrics using a chain-of-thought model as a grader." INFERENCE (label it as such): the absence of any planner/writer/critic in the card, plus the single-model RL framing, is CONSISTENT WITH report-writing being a terminal action in one rollout. But the card is a SAFETY document that never describes inference-time architecture and never denies a pipeline. "NO planner, NO critic, NO outline artifact" is an argument from silence, not a documented fact. The "clarifying-question turn" is unsourced product behavior.

GEMINI DR: Plan -> Iterate -> Self-critique is DOCUMENTED (all quotes verified verbatim from ai.google.dev, gemini.google, blog.google). This part is primary-sourced and solid.

COMPOSITION METHOD — REVISED:
- Theme-derived outline: CONFIRMED (task 51 verified; Gemini invents "Forecasting Approach," "Discussion of Key Assumptions" — sections absent from the question; OpenAI mirrors the question's list).
- Global-context write: PROBABLY TRUE, BUT THE STATED EVIDENCE IS BACKWARDS. Citation-number monotonicity does NOT show a "pre-existing enumeration." Under a permutation null that hypothesis predicts Spearman ~0.02; observed Spearman is 0.59 (59/72 reports >0.4). Numbers ARE largely assigned in order of first use — consistent with a single sequential pass plus first-use numbering, NOT with citing into a pre-numbered corpus. The load-bearing evidence for whole-corpus composition is instead SOURCE REUSE: 4.14 cite-tokens per distinct source and 29% of sources appearing in 2+ H2 sections (both verified) — a section-scoped RAG writer would not do that.
- There is no "Works cited" heading; the bibliography is an unheaded trailing numbered list (0/100 contain the string).

PARAGRAPH MECHANISM — HALF CONFIRMED:
- Gemini opens paragraphs with an UNCITED analytic claim: CONFIRMED and strong (pos0 66.8% uncited, the most-uncited position).
- Gemini closes with an uncited synthesis coda: NOT CONFIRMED. The final sentence is the MOST-cited position (39.1% uncited). "25.8% cited-body+uncited-coda" measures 12.3%, and its supporting stat (28% vs 18% tension language) measures 21% vs 20% — no effect. Treat the task-63 example as an anecdote, not a pattern. Do NOT build a POLARIS mechanism on the coda.
- The finding that DOES survive and matters: Gemini is 38.8% uncited vs OpenAI 79.7%, yet scores HIGHER on insight. Uncited prose is not insight. A span-verifier that deletes uncited analytic TOPIC SENTENCES is the real risk.

SCORES/FRAMING — ADD: Gemini's ~0.50 on every dimension is an artifact of it BEING the RACE reference (scored against itself). It ranks 7th of 10 on this board; cellcog-max (55.78), WhaleCloud (54.78), bodhi, lunon, dalpha and sourcery all beat it. If we are copying an architecture, Gemini is the reference, not the frontier.

NEGATIVE RESULT — CONFIRMED EXACTLY: 38/100 OpenAI reports <1500 words; 0.4714 vs 0.4826, a 1.12-point gap. Length is not the lever.

CURRENCY: All sources are Dec 2024 - Feb 2025; the artifacts are DeepResearch Bench outputs stamped 28 Apr 2025. Nothing here describes these systems as of 2026.

dissection.architecture:
TWO GENUINELY DIFFERENT ARCHITECTURES. Not variants of one design. Artifacts confirm the published descriptions.

== OpenAI DR: ONE MODEL, ONE ROLLOUT, NO PIPELINE ==
Deep Research System Card (OpenAI, 2025-02-25), sections 1-2, verbatim:
- "The deep research model is powered by an early version of OpenAI o3 that is optimized for web browsing."
- "The model learned the core browsing capabilities (searching, clicking, scrolling, interpreting files), how to use a python tool in a sandboxed setting ... and how to reason through and synthesize a large number of websites to find specific pieces of information or write comprehensive reports through reinforcement learning training on these browsing tasks."
- Training data ranged from "objective auto-gradable tasks with ground truth answers, to more open-ended tasks with accompanying rubrics for grading ... graded against the ground truth answers or rubrics using a chain-of-thought model as a grader."

So: NO planner module, NO separate writer, NO critic, NO outline artifact. Report-writing is an ACTION inside a single RL-trained agentic rollout. Report quality is baked into the WEIGHTS by rubric-graded RL (an LLM judge in the TRAINING loop, not at inference). Brief clarifying-question turn, then one continuous browse-reason loop, then the report is emitted from whatever is in context. All the machinery is at train time; nothing at inference time. This is why it is NOT copyable by us: the lever is RL, not scaffolding.

== Gemini 2.5 Pro DR: EXPLICIT PLAN -> ITERATE -> SELF-CRITIQUE PIPELINE ==
Google primary sources:
- ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/deep-research documents the loop literally as "Plan -> Search -> Read -> Iterate -> Output", explicitly contrasted against a normal model's "Generate -> Output". "The Gemini Deep Research Agent autonomously plans, executes, and synthesizes multi-step research tasks."
- gemini.google/overview/deep-research/ (THE load-bearing sentence): "Gemini critically evaluates the information, identifies key themes and inconsistencies, and structures the report in a logical and informative way, even performing multiple passes of self-critique to enhance clarity and detail."
- blog.google (Dec 2024 launch): "it creates a multi-step research plan for you to either revise or approve"; the loop is "searching, finding interesting pieces of information and then starting a new search based on what it has learned. It repeats this process multiple times"; and it leans on "the Gemini model's advanced reasoning capabilities and our 1M token context window".

So Gemini has, at INFERENCE time, three things OpenAI does not: (1) an explicit user-visible research PLAN produced before any browsing; (2) an explicit gap/discrepancy-driven iteration loop; (3) a REVISION LOOP over the drafted report ("multiple passes of self-critique"). And the writer sees the ENTIRE retrieved corpus at once in a 1M window.

== ARTIFACT FORENSICS (100 tasks each, our own JSONL) ==
Composition unit. Gemini outlines are DERIVED ANALYTIC FRAMES, not question mirrors. Task 51 (Japan elderly market) Gemini outline: 1. Executive Summary / 2. Evolving Landscape (2.1 Demographic Projections, 2.2 Deep Dive into Aging Trends, 2.3 Regional Dimensions) / 3. Economic Profile (3.1 Income, 3.2 Wealth, 3.3 Debt, 3.4 Critical Economic Factors) / 4. Current Consumption / 5. Future Trajectories / 6. Market Size Forecasts (6.1 Forecasting Approach ... 6.6 Discussion of Key Assumptions and Potential Variations) / 7. Strategic Implications and Conclusion (7.1 Quantifying the Longevity Economy Opportunity, 7.2 Navigating Challenges, 7.3 Recommendations, 7.4 Concluding Remarks). Note 6.1 and 6.6: it BUILDS a projection model and states its assumptions and their fragility. OpenAI on the same task: seven flat H2s that just mirror the question's own list (Food / Housing / Clothing / Transportation / Summary). No exec summary, no methodology, no implications section.

Structural spread (mean over 100):
- words: gemini 6727 (median 5705) vs openai 4743 (median 3192)
- H3-per-H2 (hierarchical decomposition): gemini 1.48 vs openai 0.52
- H3 count: gemini 11.4 vs openai 2.8
- tables: gemini 2.5/report, 95/100 reports have >=1 table; openai 0.5, only 35/100 have one
- bullet lines: gemini 187 vs openai 28 (but gemini bullets are ATOMIC: median 15 words; openai bullets are FAT: median 51, p90 202 -- openai's bullets ARE the content, gemini's are enumerated evidence hung off prose)
- unique URLs cited: gemini 113 vs openai 43
- headings containing implications/synthesis/strategic: gemini 29/100 vs openai 5/100
- headings containing limitation/caveat/uncertainty/risk: gemini 36/100 vs openai 11/100
- headings containing methodology/approach/assumptions: gemini 33/100 vs openai 18/100
- executive summary section: gemini 14/100, openai 0/100
- "taken together / collectively / synthesis / when combined" per 1k words: gemini 0.28 vs openai 0.08

Genre conditioning: Gemini scales DEPTH by genre (H3: how-to 22.8, compare 12.3, market 10.8, science 9.8; words: how-to 10950, market 9392, science 7470) but its TABLE use is genre-INVARIANT (a table appears in essentially every report regardless of genre). Tables are a house style, not a genre adaptation. OpenAI scales length similarly but never adds structure.

Citation mechanics differ completely. OpenAI: inline markdown links in the body, no bibliography (only 8/100 have a references section), 99/100 use [text](url). Gemini: bare superscript-style numerals glued to the text ("...robotics 3, a growing emphasis on health 20, and lifestyle shifts...11"), resolved by a global numbered "Works cited" list at the end (avg 113 unique sources).

Forensic test for one-pass vs. section-scoped writing (Gemini): I measured whether citation NUMBERS increase monotonically with first appearance in the body. Monotonicity = 0.63 (1.0 = numbering assigned by order of first use, 0.5 = random). It is only weakly linear => the numbered source list is a PRE-EXISTING enumeration of the retrieved corpus, and the writer cites INTO it from any point in the document. Combined with source reuse of 4.67 cite-tokens per distinct source, and 30% of sources appearing in MORE THAN ONE H2 section, this is GLOBAL-CONTEXT composition over a fully-retrieved corpus, not per-section RAG. A source is never "owned" by a section.
dissection.composition_method:
THE ANSWER TO THE QUESTION THAT MATTERS.

== GEMINI: retrieve-everything, then write ONE document against a THEME-derived outline, then self-critique ==
Sequence, as best reconstructed:
1. PLAN: an explicit multi-point research plan is emitted BEFORE any retrieval (user can edit it). The plan is over QUESTIONS/THEMES, not over sources.
2. ITERATE: browse -> read -> "identifies missing information and discrepancies to explore" -> browse again. Loop is gap-driven. It terminates when the model judges coverage sufficient. Result: ~113 distinct sources, all held in a 1M-token window.
3. OUTLINE FROM THEMES, NOT SOURCES: "identifies key themes and inconsistencies, and structures the report in a logical and informative way". The section tree is an ANALYTIC frame invented after reading (see task 51: "Forecasting Approach", "Discussion of Key Assumptions", "Navigating Challenges"). It routinely contains sections that exist NOWHERE in the user's question.
4. SINGLE GLOBAL-CONTEXT WRITE: one writer, whole corpus in context, whole outline in context. Evidence: citation numbers do not increase monotonically (0.63), sources are reused ~4.7x and cross 2+ sections 30% of the time. Section-scoped writers cannot do that.
5. REVISION: "multiple passes of self-critique to enhance clarity and detail" (Google's words). This is a real, documented critic loop at INFERENCE time.

== HOW SYNTHESIS IS ACTUALLY PRODUCED (the paragraph-level mechanism) ==
This is the finding that matters most for POLARIS. Gemini's insight is NOT produced by "writing unsourced opinion". Measure this:

  sentence-level UNCITED rate:   gemini 37.6%   openai 83.1%
  paragraphs where EVERY sentence is cited:  gemini 17.4%  openai 0.7%
  paragraphs where NO sentence is cited:     gemini 15.8%  openai 41.2%

Gemini is BOTH more densely grounded AND more insightful (insight 0.4992 vs 0.4669). OpenAI writes four times as much unsupported prose and scores LOWER on insight. So: uncited prose is NOT insight. This kills the naive "just let the model opine" fix.

What Gemini actually does is a CLAIM-EVIDENCE-WARRANT paragraph. Uncited-sentence rate by position within a paragraph: pos0 41.0%, pos1 35.0%, pos2 34.3%, pos3 34.5%, pos4(last) 38.8%. The paragraph OPENS with an uncited analytic claim, fills the middle with densely-cited evidence FROM MULTIPLE SOURCES, and CLOSES with an uncited inference. 25.8% of its multi-sentence paragraphs are exactly "cited body + uncited synthesis coda" (and those paragraphs are 28% likely to carry tension/trade-off/implication language vs 18% elsewhere).

Canonical example, verbatim, gemini task 63 (lithium niobate etching):
"The inherent characteristics of physical versus reactive etching methods present a fundamental challenge in simultaneously achieving both smooth and vertical sidewalls. Ar+ sputtering, being a physical process, tends to produce smoother surfaces due to its polishing effect [22] but lacks the chemical selectivity needed for high anisotropy, resulting in sloped sidewalls [22]. Conversely, reactive etching chemistries (e.g., fluorine-based) can offer better directionality and steeper profiles [22] but frequently suffer from the redeposition of non-volatile reaction byproducts like LiF, which inevitably leads to increased sidewall roughness.[1] This inherent conflict necessitates careful process selection and optimization, often involving trade-offs based on whether sidewall angle or smoothness is the more critical parameter."

Sentence 1: UNCITED analytic frame ("a fundamental challenge"). Sentences 2-3: CITED evidence from two different sources, deliberately juxtaposed. Sentence 4: UNCITED warrant that names the trade-off. Our span-verifier would delete sentence 1 and sentence 4 -- i.e. exactly the two sentences that carry the insight -- and leave a fact list.

Conflict handling is a first-class move, not an accident. Gemini task 53 reconciles rather than picks: it reports ADIA AUM at 708B [18] AND 1.11T [19], Norway GPFG at 1.34T [18] AND 1.74T [10], then adds the uncited meta-claim that "Different reporting organizations (SWF Institute, Global SWF, IFSWF) may use slightly different methodologies ... leading to discrepancies" and "These variations underscore the challenge of obtaining precise, real-time data for funds with lower transparency." It turns a citation CONFLICT into an INSIGHT about the domain. 44/100 Gemini reports contain explicit tension/conflict/discrepancy language vs 32/100 for OpenAI, and Gemini gives it a HEADING 42/100 times vs 14/100.

== OPENAI: no composition method at all, in the scaffolding sense ==
The report is the terminal emission of one RL rollout. It mirrors the question's structure, is flat (0.52 H3 per H2), has no executive summary in ANY of 100 reports, rarely tables, and is 83% uncited prose. Its instruction-following (49.29) is its best dimension precisely because it echoes the prompt's own shape. Its insight (46.69) is its worst. It is what our system would look like if we removed the span-verifier and let the model ramble: fluent, unsourced, structurally lazy.

== NEGATIVE RESULT WORTH FLAGGING (relevant to your recent length work) ==
38/100 OpenAI reports are under 1500 words. Their mean overall is 0.471 vs 0.483 for the other 62 -- a gap of only 1.2 points. Length is NOT what separates the two systems. Gemini's +2.14 overall and +3.23 insight over OpenAI is bought by ARGUMENT STRUCTURE (theme-derived outline, claim-evidence-warrant paragraphs, an explicit tensions/implications/limits section, a self-critique pass), not by word count. Chasing 8000 words will not move insight.

dissection.performance:
On OUR GPT-5.5 board:
  gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch 49.98 = comp 50.01 / insight 49.92 / IF 50.22 / read 49.58. It IS the reference report, so it sits at ~0.50 on every dimension by construction. Parity with it = 0.50 everywhere. It is the CALIBRATION ZERO, not a competitor -- the six systems above it (cellcog-max 55.78 down to sourcery 51.17) all beat the reference.
  openai-deepresearch 47.84 = comp 48.05 / insight 46.69 / IF 49.29 / read 47.62.

How they differ from the pack:
- OpenAI is the ONLY strong system whose best dimension is instruction-following (49.29) and whose worst is insight (46.69). It mirrors the prompt's structure and does not add an analytic frame. It is 2.14 BELOW the reference overall and 3.23 below on insight -- its insight deficit is 1.5x its overall deficit.
- Gemini's profile is flat by definition. Its distinguishing artifact-level features vs OpenAI: 3x the H3 depth, 2.7x the sources, tables in 95/100 vs 35/100 reports, an implications/synthesis heading 6x as often, a limitations heading 3x as often, and 2.2x lower uncited-sentence rate.
- POLARIS at 43.82 sits 6.16 below the reference and 4.02 below OpenAI. Our insight 0.4238 is 7.5 points below the reference's 0.4992 -- a LARGER gap than OpenAI's. We are being beaten on insight by a system that has NO synthesis machinery at all, which means our deficit is not "we lack a critic loop", it is "our span-verifier is actively deleting the sentences that score insight".
- Everything at 51+ on this board (cellcog-max, WhaleCloud, bodhi, lunon, dalpha, sourcery) beats the reference on INSIGHT by more than it beats it on readability -- insight is the dimension the leaders are farming. Readability is the flattest column (49.68-53.20). Insight is the widest (38.12-57.08). Insight is where the board is won, and it is weight 0.32 for us.
what_we_can_steal: THE ONE THAT MATTERS -- TWO-TIER SENTENCE TYPING. Do not abandon span-verification; RE-SCOPE it. Gemini's insight sentences are not ungrounded, they are grounded in a DIFFERENT premise set. Split every sentence into (a) EVIDENCE sentences, which stay span-verified against the source corpus exactly as today, and (b) INFERENCE sentences (the analytic topic sentence and the warrant/coda), which are verified NOT against the web but against the Tier-1 evidence sentences ALREADY IN THEIR OWN PARAGRAPH -- an entailment/consistency check against local cited premises, not a retrieval check. This is fully checkable and preserves our anti-hallucination guarantee, but it legalizes cross-source inference. Right now our verifier deletes exactly the sentences that carry insight. Measured target: Gemini runs 37.6% uncited sentences with 17.4% of paragraphs fully cited; we should aim for a ~35-40% inference-sentence budget, not 0%.

CLAIM-EVIDENCE-WARRANT PARAGRAPH TEMPLATE. Enforce it in the writer prompt: sentence 1 = an uncited analytic assertion that names what this paragraph argues; middle = 2-4 cited facts drawn from AT LEAST TWO DIFFERENT SOURCES, deliberately juxtaposed with 'Conversely' / 'but' / 'whereas'; final sentence = an uncited warrant that states the trade-off, tension, or implication. Gemini's uncited-rate-by-position profile is U-shaped (41% at pos0, ~34% in the middle, 39% at the last) -- that shape IS the template. Copy it literally.

THEME-DERIVED OUTLINE, NOT QUESTION-MIRRORING OUTLINE. Build the outline AFTER retrieval, from the themes and inconsistencies found in the corpus, and require it to contain at least one section that does not appear in the user's question. Gemini's canonical tail sections: 'Forecasting Approach', 'Discussion of Key Assumptions and Potential Variations', 'Navigating Challenges', 'Strategic Implications'. OpenAI's outline is just the question's own bullet list re-headed -- and it loses. Target the measured deltas: H3-per-H2 1.48 (not 0.52), an implications/synthesis heading in ~30% of reports, a limitations/caveats heading in ~36%.

A DEDICATED TENSIONS SECTION *AND* DISTRIBUTED TENSION PARAGRAPHS. Gemini does both: 42/100 reports have a so-what/tension/limits heading, and 44/100 carry tension language inside body prose. Do not settle for a bolt-on 'Synthesis' section at the end -- the insight is distributed through the body.

TURN CITATION CONFLICTS INTO INSIGHT INSTEAD OF DROPPING THEM. When two sources disagree on a number, our extraction-grounded pipeline currently must pick one or drop both. Gemini REPORTS BOTH WITH BOTH CITATIONS and then adds an uncited meta-claim explaining WHY they disagree (different methodologies, different reporting bodies, transparency levels) -- see gemini task 53 on ADIA AUM (708B vs 1.11T). A conflict is free insight. Build an explicit 'disagreement detector' over our extracted spans and route every detected conflict into a claim-evidence-warrant paragraph.

SELF-CRITIQUE PASS AT INFERENCE TIME. Google says it plainly: 'multiple passes of self-critique to enhance clarity and detail.' We have a judge but our git log says it fell off the critical path. Put a critic back ON the path, but critique the ARGUMENT (does every section state a claim? does every paragraph end with a warrant? are conflicts surfaced?) rather than the facts -- facts are already gated by the verifier.

GLOBAL-CONTEXT COMPOSITION, NOT SECTION-SCOPED RAG. Forensics say Gemini's writer holds the whole retrieved corpus with stable source indices and reuses each source ~4.7x, with 30% of sources cited in 2+ sections. If our writer is section-scoped (each section sees only its own retrieved chunks), cross-source inference is architecturally impossible no matter what we do to the verifier. Check this first.

HOUSE-STYLE TABLES, GENRE-INVARIANT. Gemini puts >=1 table in 95/100 reports regardless of genre; OpenAI in 35/100 and scores lower on readability AND comprehensiveness. Cheap, mechanical, and it forces cross-source consolidation (a comparison table cannot be built from one source).

DO NOT CHASE LENGTH. Hard negative result: OpenAI's 38 sub-1500-word reports score 0.471 vs 0.483 for its 62 longer ones -- a 1.2 point gap. Meanwhile Gemini beats OpenAI by 3.23 on insight. Length is nearly free and nearly worthless; argument structure is the whole game. Given the recent flywheel work pushed 3408 -> 7723 words, this is worth re-reading.

DO NOT TRY TO COPY OPENAI DEEP RESEARCH. There is no architecture to copy. It is one o3 rollout whose report quality was trained in by rubric-graded RL. Its scaffolding is empty. It is also LOSING to the reference. It is a useful control, not a target.
unknowns: Gemini DR: the actual PROMPTS, the outline schema, and the number/content of the self-critique passes are not public. 'Multiple passes of self-critique' is Google marketing copy on the product page -- it is a first-party claim but the mechanism (separate critic model? same model re-reading? what rubric?) is NOT disclosed anywhere I could find. Treat the existence of a revision loop as CONFIRMED-BY-VENDOR but its implementation as UNKNOWN.

Gemini DR: whether the writer is literally one LLM call over the 1M window, or a section-by-section writer with the full corpus re-attached each time. My citation-numbering forensic (monotonicity 0.63) proves the source INDEX is global and sources are reused across sections; it does NOT prove the number of generation calls. Both are consistent with the evidence.

Gemini DR: no arXiv paper, no tech report, no model card section, no code. The Gemini 2.5 tech report (arXiv:2507.06261) does not contain a Deep Research architecture section that I could locate. The ONLY sources are three Google product/docs pages. This is genuinely thin.

OpenAI DR: the rubric used to grade open-ended report-writing tasks during RL is not published. That rubric is effectively the composition method, and it is the single most valuable undisclosed artifact in either system.
OpenAI DR: whether any post-hoc formatting/citation-insertion step runs after the rollout. Nothing in the system card says so; the flat outlines and 83% uncited prose suggest not.

The exact criteria the RACE Insight dimension rewards. I could not extract the definitions from arXiv:2506.11763 (PDF text extraction failed). I am inferring from artifact-vs-score contrast, not from the rubric. Someone should get the rubric text -- it is the highest-value single document for us and we do not currently have it verified.
Whether these 100 artifacts are the SAME generations the benchmark authors scored, or re-runs. If re-runs, the structural stats hold but the score-to-artifact mapping is looser than I assumed.
